reviews.text: The best home stay we have been to in a long time. Beautiful location, amazing surroundings, and such a welcoming family. The children didn't feel like leaving this place. Food was home cooked and was very delicious. I would strongly recommend this place to anyone looking for a quiet stay in the lap of nature.
reviews.author: Vishal Arya

reviews.text:
A Hidden Gem for Nature Lovers

Our recent stay at this homestay was nothing short of magical. Nestled in a stunning location, the property is surrounded by pristine nature and offers an organic, tranquil retreat. The 30-minute hike to reach the property adds to the charm, making you feel like you've left the hustle of everyday life far behind.

The food was an absolute highlight – a true "farm-to-plate" experience. The authentic Chamba dishes, prepared on a traditional chulha, were not only delicious but also heartwarming. Each meal was served with humility and accompanied by the warm company of the host family, making the experience even more special.

Our child had an incredible time playing with the host family's children and the caretakers. Their kindness and hospitality created a welcoming and inclusive environment that felt like home.

The host and their family were humble, passionate, and truly invested in ensuring our stay was memorable. Their love for their land and culture shone through in every interaction.

If you're looking for an authentic and enriching experience, this homestay is a must-visit. It's a beautiful blend of nature, culture, and heartfelt hospitality that will leave you rejuvenated and inspired.
reviews.author: Prachi Dubey

saratha-vilas.editorial: Opulent mansion-turned-boutique hotel offering 8 upscale rooms, an outdoor pool & free breakfast.

reviews.text: Saratha Vilas is a superb experience not to be missed. The Chettinad region is a very culturally rich, welcoming area with amazing architecture, temples, and villages not spoiled by mass tourism. The owners of Saratha have lovingly restored the mansion in a refined yet unstuffy manner and set the tone for their whole professional team.  All offer guests privacy but should you wish to engage, you will be rewarded with intelligent conversation filled with good humor, local knowledge, and an authentic zest for cultural exchange!  The food is prepared and served with pride in lovely surroundings.  An in house art collection is fascinating to browse and the garden/pool an oasis for guests and the charming rose ringed parakeets aloft!  We were glad we settled in for 6 nights to avail ourselves of many options the area has to offer. An excellent in house brochure as well as personalized logistics support enhanced our stay. The guiding provided was really great, giving us a lens to better understand the area (thanks Nachi!). The warmth of this staff is hard to sum up in a review  All told, Saratha is not just a hotel, but an experience which will leave you with not only photos, but indelible memories.
reviews.author: Chris Sondermann

reviews.text:
Our stay at Saratha Vilas was one of those rare travel moments that feels truly special from beginning to end. The house itself is absolutely beautiful,elegant, full of character, and surrounded by a peaceful atmosphere that immediately makes you slow down and breathe differently. Every corner tells a story, and you really feel the soul of the place.

One of the highlights of our stay was the genuine sense of welcome we felt throughout our time there. Conversations flowed naturally, and there was a rare generosity in the way the place is shared with guests, simple, sincere, and unforgettable.

The food deserves a special mention, it was, without question, the best we had during our entire trip. Every meal was refined, generous, and full of flavor.

A truly special place that we would recommend without hesitation.
reviews.author: Céline Blomme

reviews.text: Absolutely fantastic place to stay! A travel worth on its own .The historic palace has been turned into a lovely hotel with incredible sensitivity by the owners. The team made us feel home from the first moment with its great friendliness. The architecture tour with Nachi exploring the nearby historic buildings was super interesting, he is such a great guide. Thanks to everyone for these two wonderful days.
reviews.author: Anke Pfef

reviews.text: We stayed at Saratha Vilas for 3 nights. It’s a Chettiar Mansion redone very tastefully preserving the cultural aspects while providing modern conveniences that foreigners and NRIs expect. The place has been renovated and managed by two French architects who reside on the premises in winter and with whom we interacted extensively. We had lunch at 3 different Mansions and I have to say that we were very pleased with the boutique nature of Saratha, the warm welcome from the staff and our delicious breakfast and dinners! Highly recommend for the ultimate Chettinad experience.
reviews.author: raj rajgopal

reviews.text: We stayed there for one night and wished he had a longer stayed there longer. The room, common areas and surrounding were very well maintained. Food was all prepared within the premises kitchen and it was tasty. The staffs were very equipped to handle any requests. Alphie, the villa manager was well aware of the historical significance of the place, owners, villa and made our stay memorable. I’ll recommend it if you are look for peace, tradition and Chettinad set up.
reviews.author: Henry Victor

maison-perumal.editorial: Elegant rooms in a Colonial-style building, plus dining, a bar & free breakfast.

reviews.text:
Went here for the restaurant. This is Indian food catered towards white people. After over a month traveling India this was the most toned down meal we had. Also the meal was supposed to come with several items that never came. When we pointed out that it was missing half the items, they brought some but not all & still charged full price :(

However the ambiance was lovely in the courtyard. This restaurant would be good for people who are very new to Indian food / prefer much milder flavours. If you’re looking for something very authentic look elsewhere.
reviews.author: Asiah Butler

reviews.text:
I am loving my stay at this small hotel in the Tamil Quarter of Pondicherry. It has a homely yet also a luxurious feel in the attention to detail such as loose leaf tea and cookies provided in the rooms, all the amenities you could need, the opportunity to create kolam designs in front of the entrance steps and experience a cookery session in the early evening (all free of charge).
The staff team are warm, welcoming and kind, and have made a positive difference to my time here.
The local cuisine is delicious and beautifully prepared with great care.
The room I have is a deluxe with four poster bed which I recommend as it has window views over the street.
From here, it is easy to walk to many of the main sites including the Sri Aurobindo ashram which is 10 minutes away.
reviews.author: Daisy Perry

reviews.text: My wife and I had lunch here. Hands down the best lunch experience of our stay at Pondicherry. While the food was really tasty, it was the ambience that made it for us. Serene, and positive vibes, it was a treat.
reviews.author: Siddharth Tanwar

reviews.text:
Had lunch at the Maison and loved the ambience. The wait time for the food was high and it was on the pricey side, but the food was really well made with high quality ingredients that made it worth it!

We had the Kaalan Milagu Pirattal and Kozhi Varutha Curry. And for dessert we had Mango Icecream.

Lovely spot for a relaxed meal amidst green and very thoughtfully planned surroundings.
reviews.author: divya sanchana

reviews.text:
Here I am sharing my view regarding the food of restaurant not about the hotel service.
This place is excellent if u are content creater/blogger/ for photos etc,coz it has great views with ambience.
But the food is only hype ,nothing is there,neither the  taste nor the quantity .as per the price,the quantity is nothing.lastly if you want good pictures for your trip to post ,definitely go but if u want to eat, as you are Hungary, choose any other place.
reviews.author: Sanjukta Behera

shahpura-haveli.editorial: Genteel rooms, some with frescoes, in an elegant heritage hotel offering restaurants & bars.

reviews.text:
It's an awesome experience and feeling to have spend some time is such a heritage property!!!

Here is what the hotel chain has to say...
Escape the ordinary and embark on a journey that transcends time and tradition with Shahpura Hotels and Resorts. Their exquisite collection of hotels offers you to a world where heritage meets luxury, and every moment is a celebration of opulence and heritage.

Imagine waking up in a 300-year-old heritage haveli, steeped in history and adorned with timeless elegance. Picture yourself indulging in the lavish comforts of a modern boutique hotel, where contemporary design seamlessly blends with old-world charm. At Shahpura Hotels and Resorts, we offer a collage of experiences that redefine luxury and hospitality.

Where they can earn more star or improve.

# The road to the property is very very narrow which needs some thoughtful consideration.
# The meals (breakfast & lunch/dinner) included must have more options if buffet is not served.
# Room service/cleaning can be better.
#door handles are very inconvenient and everytime hurts the hand... no space for even fingers in between.
# shower should have a curtain when the toilet is attached.
reviews.author: Madhavi Shah

reviews.text:
We had a wonderful stay at Shahpura Haveli in Shahpura. The place has a beautiful village vibe, surrounded by hills and greenery that make it peaceful and relaxing. The terrace offers a stunning sunset view that we truly enjoyed.

Instead of a regular buffet, they offered freshly prepared food with good choices, which felt more personal and authentic. The staff were cooperative and very welcoming throughout our stay.

It’s also very kids-friendly, and our family had a great time exploring the heritage property. Overall, a lovely experience combining heritage charm, nature, and warm hospitality.
reviews.author: Deepak Mishra

reviews.text:
Palace of Soul, Beauty, and Exceptional Hospitality!

From the very first moment, our stay at Shaipur Haveli felt truly special. We were warmly welcomed by Dharmendra Singh, whose genuine hospitality immediately set the tone. He kindly took the time to give us a personal tour through the entire property, sharing the history and details of this beautifully restored palace with great passion.

The haveli itself is simply stunning. Every corner reflects thoughtful restoration and attention to detail. The rooms are individually designed, each with its own unique color palette and character, creating a sense of charm and authenticity that is rare to find.

The garden is a peaceful oasis—lovingly maintained, lush, and perfectly inviting. The pool area, surrounded by greenery, offers a serene retreat and is ideal for relaxing after a day of exploring.

What truly elevates the experience is the exceptional service. The team is attentive, warm, and effortlessly professional, making you feel cared for at all times without ever being intrusive.

This is not just a place to stay—it is an experience. I would return here without hesitation and very much look forward to coming back on my next trip to India.

Thank you so much!
reviews.author: Sandra Schüssler

reviews.text:
Fabulous lunch in this rural setting, music dancing and four courses of Indian specialities.  Dharmendra looked after our every need and was professional and efficient.
Great experience to break our journey from Delhi to Jaipur
reviews.author: claire cochran

reviews.text:
A Soulful Royal Experience – Where History Meets Heartfelt Hospitality

Our recent stay at Shahpura Haveli was not just a holiday; it was a deep immersion into the royal heritage of Rajasthan. As a family that values our culture and traditions, we found this property to be a sanctuary of grace and warmth.

🏛️ Property, History & Architecture

The Haveli is a 300-year-old masterpiece that has been preserved with incredible love. The architectural details—from the hand-painted frescoes to the stone-carved balconies—are breathtaking.

The Ambiance: The decoration strikes a perfect balance between antique charm and modern comfort.

Facilities: The property is well-equipped with a beautiful Swimming Pool nestled in the courtyard, a grand Reception, a sophisticated Bar, and a majestic Dining Hall. They also have ample Parking, which is very convenient.

The Rooms: Living in the rooms feels like being a part of history, with high ceilings and royal interiors that offer a peaceful retreat.

👑 The Personal Touch of the Royal Family
The highlight of my stay was meeting Mr. Digraj Singh Shahpura ("Bade Saab"). Despite being a member of the Royal Family, his down-to-earth nature and dedication to his heritage are truly inspiring. Seeing him personally oversee the Haveli in the evenings shows his immense commitment to his work and culture.

👨‍🍳 Culinary Excellence & Service
Being a vegetarian family, we were delighted by the food.

Master Chef Abdul: A true magician in the kitchen. Every meal was flavorful, authentic, and prepared with great care. After serving each food he personally visit  to us and get feedback and even get order for next food personally.

Management: Mr. Vijender ji, the caretaker, manages the property with perfection. He ensured our experience was flawless and guided us through the best the Haveli has to offer.

Staff: A special shoutout to Ajay and the other young team member in Room Service. They didn't just serve us; they treated us like family members of royal family.

🐎 Royal Experiences & Activities

Mr. Vijender ji organized a trip to the Local Bazaar and Horse Farm in the hotel’s custom-made special Jeep. It was a "Feel Like Royal" moment that my family will never forget and A visit to the farm to see the majestic Marwari horses is a must-do for any guest.

Bonfire Dinner: We had a special Bonfire Dinner which was the perfect setting for family bonding under the stars.

Final Thought
Shahpura Haveli is not just a hotel; it is an emotion. I highly recommend this property to everyone. Whether it is for the history, the food, or the incredible hospitality of "Bade Saab" and his team, this is a place you will want to visit again and again.

Jai Shri Krishna!
reviews.author: Rajnish Kumar

reviews.text: Best school for its area.  Your expectations will be completed in this school about your children's
reviews.author: Vishnu Sagar

reviews.text: Best school for bright future
reviews.author: Abhishek Sharma

reviews.text: Best place for Smart Education
reviews.author: Krishna Kumar

hilltop-castle-udaipur.editorial: Casual hotel offering low-key rooms, plus a rooftop restaurant, an outdoor pool & a bar.

reviews.text:
Extremely disappointing experience so far at Hilltop Palace. We have booked a Deluxe Lake Facing Room (Room 414) and paid extra specifically expecting a premium lake-facing stay. Unfortunately, apart from the lake view, almost everything else about the room has been a major letdown.

The maintenance and hygiene standards are shockingly poor for a hotel that markets itself as a premium lake-facing property. The pillow covers and towels have visible stains, which is completely unacceptable in any hotel charging these prices.

However, the most disturbing part of the experience is the washroom. It is extremely old, poorly maintained and gives a very unhygienic impression. The toilet seat looks outdated and dirty, the mirror is stained, and the tiles are old and grimy. The entire bathroom feels like something from a decades-old budget lodge rather than a “Deluxe” category room.

What makes this even more frustrating is that the photos shown on booking platforms display modern, clean and well-maintained washrooms, which is clearly not what guests are actually being given. The reality of the room is drastically different from what is being advertised online, which feels misleading and unfair to guests who are paying a premium expecting a certain standard.

Frankly speaking, the washroom in my own home is in far better condition than what is being offered here as a deluxe room.

I am currently staying at the property and will be here for the next 3 days, so I sincerely hope the management takes this review seriously and addresses these issues immediately. A hotel with such a fantastic location and lake view should not be delivering such poorly maintained rooms.

Right now, it feels like guests are being charged premium prices purely for the view while the actual room quality and upkeep fall far below basic expectations.
reviews.author: Muskaan Gupta

reviews.text:
Had a great stay at Hilltop Palace Hotel Udaipur. The location is amazing — it’s near Fateh Sagar Lake and very easy to find since the hotel sits at one of the highest points around the lake.

The view is the highlight of this property. From here you can enjoy a stunning panoramic view of the lake and the entire Udaipur city. If you book the lake-view rooms, the experience becomes even more special — the view from the rooms is truly excellent.

Overall, a very good stay with one of the best viewpoints in Udaipur. ⭐⭐⭐⭐
reviews.author: Yuvrajsinh Solanki

reviews.text:
We booked for a day,
Room was clean and spacious.
We had a semi lake view room.
Even the washrooms were huge.
Loved the amenities.
The receptionist was very friendly and kind.
The morning buffet was 10/10 loved it.
Even they have a terrace from where you can have a view of the beautiful sunset.
Totally recommend.
reviews.author: Parth Singh Parihar

reviews.text:
We came here for the first time to stay. Usually, we prefer lakeside stays, but we had a good experience here.
On the first day, rooms were not available, so we stayed in a deluxe non-lake-facing room. The room felt small and a bit congested. Otherwise, it was clean, had a balcony, and the washroom was also clean.
For the next stay, we had a deluxe lake-facing room, and we enjoyed it much more. It was very spacious, and you could see Fateh Sagar directly even while lying on the bed. The balcony was also large with a sitting arrangement. We visited at the end of June, and because of the rain, the atmosphere became even more pleasant. You can also see Monsoon Palace from the room.
The poolside area is good, but compared to other hotels, it felt average. The outside sitting arrangement was very nice, and you get a great view of Fateh Sagar from there.
At night, we had dinner at the in-house restaurant. The taste was good, but the prices felt a bit high. In the morning, the buffet breakfast had a variety of options and tasted good as well.
Fateh Sagar is very close from there. Other places are also nearby, but if you don’t want to take an auto, you’ll have to walk to the main road.
reviews.author: Aayushi Patel

reviews.text:
Hilltop Palace, Udaipur – Royal Stay with Lake Views

Hilltop Palace offers a truly royal and relaxing experience. The rooms are spacious and aesthetic, and if you opt for a lake-facing balcony, the views are absolutely worth it. Do note: lake-facing rooms are premium, and it’s best to book at least a month in advance.

The lobby and garden area overlooking the lake is the most interesting part of the property—perfect for relaxing and easily one of the best picture-taking spots. Breakfast here is a must, with a good spread and a peaceful start to the day.

The rooftop dining area doubles as a sunset point, complete with a bar, making evenings magical. Car parking is sufficient, and most major tourist attractions are nearby, which makes sightseeing convenient.

Overall, Hilltop Palace is a great choice if you’re looking for comfort, views, and classic Udaipur charm.
reviews.author: Owais Shaikh

reviews.text:
I went here after being referred by a friend.
Situated in a nice cosy area with beautiful views overlooking the hills and there is a very famous attraction in a walkable distance named chimney.
The food was beautiful and the uncle and aunty who hosts the home stay is beautiful by heart ♥️🙌such a lovely experience it was.
I been to many places and have stayed in so many places but for some reason my heart is already pushing me to back to this place
Just because the hospitality was magical ..
thanks and see you soon 🙌
And forgot to mention it’s very clean and very well maintained.
reviews.author: nikhil alias

reviews.text: Homestay is absolutely mesmerizing. The view that one can get is amazing.  The property is amazing and very very clean. Rooms are enough specious with beautifully decorated.  Owner and his wife both of them are very very polite. Food is very tasty. Their hospitality is simply amazing. We stayed there for one night and in the morning I have captured the sunrise even the mighty kanchanjunga. Very peaceful homestay. They provide 24*7 water supply as well as hot water and they have all time available wifi. Must recommended homestay.
reviews.author: Shovantanu Joarder

reviews.text: We stayed here from 18.05.26 to 20.05.26. It was an wonderful experience. Hosts are fantastic, foods were very good. The place is so beautiful!
reviews.author: Manas Patra

reviews.text: I must say the room the view the service everything are awesome, it literally feel like a home . Aunty and uncle they are so nice people so humble also and aunty ka haath ka khana so testy ❤❤❤❤ I recommend everyone too must visit🥰🥰🥰🥰
reviews.author: Roma Besra

reviews.text: It's a very great homestay...it offers a great view,and people here are really humble polite and very gentle...they really care about our comfort...and the view from here is mind blowing.❤️
reviews.author: Krish Zaman

reviews.text: Erstwhile palace of The Venganad Nambitis of Kollengode - Kollengode Kovilakam - is a  must-see historic building. Now its "Kalari Kovilakam" - an Ayurvedic resort operated by CGH Earth Experience Hotels Kochi.
reviews.author: dotcompals

reviews.text:
Kalari Kovilakam was a palace, now am ayurvedic hospital offering healthcare and ayurvedic treatments based on ancient Indian system of healing. This was the place where first Chinmaya Vidyalaya was held. Kovilakam temple is opened for visitors during the Kollengode Aarattu.
Also, I've been here for few Violin and music concerts. It was a great experience:))))
reviews.author: Avanthika S Nambiar

reviews.text:
I arrived at Kalari Kovilakom on Easter Day with high expectations, only to be met with a truly disappointing experience. During my very first massage session—meant to mark the beginning of a healing journey—I was subjected to loud construction drilling noises that completely ruined the atmosphere and made it impossible to relax.

Despite several requests to address the issue, the noise continued throughout the entire day. Eventually, the General Manager admitted they were behind on construction work, but offered no apology or gesture to acknowledge the disruption and discomfort caused.

For a place that positions itself as a sanctuary for peace and restoration, this was completely unacceptable. A deeply disheartening start to what was supposed to be a transformative stay.
reviews.author: tatiana borysova

reviews.text: Service is amazing but need to recruit more Ayurveda doctors in palace. Please 🙏 consider it
reviews.author: Hindu Pillai

reviews.text: This palace is built by Kollengode Raja in 1904 and gave it to his daughter. In 1975, the Department of Archeology acquired the property and converted it into a museum. Now Kalari Kovilakom is one of the finest Ayurvedic retreats in the state of Kerala. Run by CGH Earth Ayurveda resorts group. Tourists come from far and wide to experience the effects of science of Ayurveda.
reviews.author: Jibin MR

nimmu-house-ladakh.editorial: Serene 1900s mountain hotel with rustic rooms & tents, plus dining, yoga & trekking.

reviews.text: Everything about this place is a 10/10. The rooms are super cute, service is great and the views from the yogashala are insane. The staff is really nice and helpful. Everything is so thoughtfully planned. The food is very heartwarming. Loved the place!
reviews.author: Aparna Gaur

reviews.text: Charming hotel, attentive staff, and excellent food made with produce from the garden. It's a true little corner of paradise. Plan to stay at least 2-3 days to fully enjoy it, especially since there are many places to visit in the surrounding area.
reviews.author: Coralie KERESTEDJIAN

reviews.text:
We stayed here for 2 nights , it is a perfect place , who wants to enjoy nature, silence . Best hospitality by Sonam , Indrani.  Best food . We required Meals without garlic and onion , that was done with perfection.
Nice place and must visit.
reviews.author: Prateek Jain

reviews.text:
A heartfelt haven in the Himalayas ..Nimmu House, Ladakh

We had the most beautiful stay at Nimmu House while acclimatizing for our Ladakh trip, and I can’t recommend it enough. From the moment we arrived, Indrani and Vikram, our gracious hosts, made us feel completely at home.
We stayed in their charming tents..comfortable, beautifully maintained, and designed with such incredible attention to detail. One of the sweetest touches was the handwritten welcome note waiting for us in the tent..it truly set the tone for the warmth and care that followed throughout our stay.
Every part of Nimmu House reflects thoughtful hospitality, from the cozy design of the space to the delicious home-cooked meals. Everything was taken care of so gently and naturally that we could truly relax and just soak in the peace of the place.
Indrani and Vikram went out of their way to ensure we had everything we needed, and it never once felt transactional..it felt personal, like staying with dear friends.
If you’re looking for a place in Ladakh that is soulful, serene, and lovingly run, Nimmu House is it. Thank you, Indrani and Vikram, for such a memorable experience..we’ll carry the feeling of your home with us for a long time to come. 🌸🌺🌸
reviews.author: Monisha Bajaj

reviews.text:
This is a hidden gem you don't want to miss in Ladakh! My stay at Nimmu House was nothing short of amazing. I'm not quite sure why they are listed as a 2 star hotel because they should be listed as a 4 or 5 stars - The location is tucked away in the quiet village of Nimmoo, which is a short 45 mins drive from Leh and you can expect to be fully immersed in nature and stillness. The grounds of the location is filled with apricot and apple trees, (Yes, we were lucky enough to eat these fresh fruits straight from the trees!) sunflowers, and all sorts of different plants. They have a beautiful courtyard where you can sit to read or enjoy their delicious farm to table meals. Everything, and I mean everything, is so fresh and delicious.

It is obvious that there is so much love, attention and care that has been put into making this place one that you'll remember. From the food, to the design of the rooms/tents, to the handwritten welcome note, to the service. Aside from the physical space, what made this experience even more incredible were the staff - A big big thank you Indrani, Vikram, Sonam and the rest of the Nimmu team. Thank you for going over and beyond to make sure that everyone was comfortable and enjoying our stay, and showing us what true hospitality means. And not forgetting the 2 resident pups Zero and Slow ❤️  I'm so grateful to have had this magical experience and will definitely be back!
reviews.author: S W

ahilya-by-the-sea.editorial: Genteel seafront hotel featuring outdoor pools, a spa & gardens, plus a restaurant with a terrace.

reviews.text:
We spent three unforgettable days here for our team offsite, and the experience was nothing short of extraordinary. From the moment we arrived, it felt like home — warm, personal, and yet luxuriously special.

What truly stood out was the hospitality and warmth of the staff. None of them are formally trained in hotel management, yet their intuitive care, attention to detail, and genuine smiles made us feel more welcome than any five-star property ever could. It was hospitality straight from the heart.

The culinary experience crafted by Chef Avinash deserves a special mention — each meal was not just food, but a thoughtfully curated experience, with spreads that delighted us every single time.

And of course, the resort itself — a beautiful blend of antique charm, rustic elegance, natural beauty, and the serene location by the sea — made it the perfect backdrop for our time together.

Ahilya by the Sea is more than a resort — it’s an experience of warmth, beauty, and soulful living. Truly unforgettable. 🌊✨🌿
reviews.author: Minakshi Sharma

reviews.text:
We stayed at Ahilya by the sea for a week - the minute we stepped into the property, we felt like this is what we needed. Everything right from the location, service, scenery, rooms was superb. Loved the whole antique artsy vibe of the place.
Chef Avinash was kind enough to accommodate all our requests on days we dined at the property. The food was delicious and made with the best local ingredients.
The staff is hands down one of the best in our experience in terms of service. They genuinely take care of you like they mean it!

Highly recommend staying here - this will be our go to place whenever we’re in Goa!
reviews.author: Nancy S M

reviews.text: Wow, this hotel is the most amazing location, and the villas and gardens are absolutely stunning. The views across the pool and garden onto the sea are amazing and you don’t get bored of them! The staff are incredibly helpful, and nothing is too much trouble, although perhaps lacked a personal friendliness that we experienced elsewhere on our trip. The staff were incredibly helpful in recommending things to do and helping with taxis etc. The sunset boat trip was a must, an hour out with local fishermen on a small boat, and the dolphins treated us to lots of jumping and dancing in the water which will live with us for a long time! The food was good without being exceptional, however the martini bar offering was brilliant. Lovely rooms with nice big comfortable beds. As a plant lover, I wandered around the garden a lot looking at Al the amazing plants. The swimming pool was also absolutely fabulous. Overall I would not hesitate in recommending this hotel, it was fantastic. I hope to return one day!
reviews.author: Jack Hinshelwood

reviews.text: Staying at Ahilya by the Sea is like stepping into a serene dream—where time slows, the sea sings softly to the shore, and every corner whispers of thoughtful elegance. The hospitality is not merely service; it is heartfelt, gracious, and deeply personal, like being welcomed into a lovely family. Each moment spent there feels wrapped in quiet luxury, where nature and comfort dance in perfect harmony. It is not just a place to stay—it is an experience to be remembered, cherished, and longed for once you've left. A true five-star haven, in every sense of the word.
reviews.author: Sangeetha Sundar

reviews.text: This is definitely the best hidden gem Goa has to offer. Nested right next to the beach, this place offers the best view. I stayed here for one night and really wished it was longer. The staff is super friendly, and chef Avinash is hands down the best - based on our taste preferences he recommended some amazing food options which truly did not disappoint at all. The rooms are very spacious and overlook the ocean. This property has really preserved their heritage and I can’t wait to go back again soon! If you are visiting Goa, please book this for the guaranteed best time!
reviews.author: Shreya Tewatia

reviews.text:
One of the most beautiful and fulfilling stays in the north. Experience the best of Tirthan Valley and experience immense peace and serenity at heart at Thehraav. We were there with a 4 yo and believe me not just us, he too was in love with the place. Aman and Kamal became his best friends along with the best hosts they already are. The place is located on the river and it's beautiful feeling to experience the sounds of gushing waters. The restaurant space feels like home where you can sit and chill for hours with really delicious food from the kitchen. The staff is so friendly and helpful and engages you as they explain and share stories about the place.
Heart is full and memories are loud and beautiful of this place. It's a great retreat for anyone who wants to experience the hills, for chilling at the room or property, for trekking around, for fishing as a few fellow traveller's were into.
reviews.author: samyak gandhi

reviews.text: Loved our stay at Thehraav! The name is so apt for the location and experience. It actually feels so tranquil, stable and a quite and slow life there. It was amazing to unwind there with my parents and husband. We just had a great time sitting by the river, enjoying bonfire in the evenings. The rooms were so cozy with the electric blankets and tall heater towers, we had gone there near to the Christmas and new year time there and it was decorated for those occasions. The staff was very cordial and loved the homely food. I would go back anyday!
reviews.author: Harman Sohi

reviews.text:
Had a great time during my stay here. The cottages are such a unique living experience, the staff is super helpful, the food is simple yet delicious and the views are amazing!
Staying at Thehraav definitely feels like a pause, and I recommend it for anyone looking for some quality time off.
reviews.author: Jatin Chakraborty

reviews.text:
The property is only good for the river view and easy access to the river, continous flowing water noice, garden to play for kids.
The food you order will take atleast 3 hours to prepare.Half of the items on menu are not available. They are always understaffed so expect a lot of delays in all services.
The owner is also not bothered about wtever complain you give or feedback.
reviews.author: harshada agrawal

reviews.text:
Had a great experience overall. The location is excellent and very convenient, and the food quality was impressive. The ambience and service made the visit even more enjoyable. What I particularly liked is that, despite the great quality and location, the pricing is quite reasonable and not overly expensive.

I truly had a wonderful time and would definitely recommend this place to others looking for a river side resort.
reviews.author: Gaurav Gulati

shakti-kumaon.editorial: Minimalist suites in an upscale hotel surrounded by mountains, plus a library & fine dining.

reviews.text:
Our stay at The Kumaon was absolutely wonderful. The property has a warm, cosy charm and is beautifully designed giving it an earthy, eco-friendly feel that blends seamlessly with the surroundings.
We had booked the Terrace Chalet room and it was superb with breathtaking Himalayan views. The food was absolutely delicious . The hospitality was exceptional throughout. A special thanks to Abhinav & team for making our stay so comfortable and memorable.
A truly beautiful retreat in the heart of the Himalayas, perfect for peace, nature, and serenity.
reviews.author: Ash Pandey

reviews.text:
We had a wonderful stay at Hotel Kumaon, and what stood out most was the genuine care from the entire team—from even before we reached the hotel, throughout the stay, and the follow-up continued until we got back home safely. That level of attention is rare and deeply appreciated.
A special thank you to the Manager, Mr. Abhinav, for handling everything so smoothly. Also, sincere thanks to Mr. Suraj, Mr. Rakesh, Mr. Naveen Adhikari, Mr. Pushkar, Mr. Rohit, and Mr. Saurav for being consistently helpful, responsive, and courteous.
The rooms were well maintained—credit to the housekeeping team, especially Mr. Nandan (Housekeeping),Mr Puran  and Mr. Gaurav. The food was excellent throughout the stay—fresh, tasty, and consistently good.
A special thank you to Chef Mr. Naveen Adhikari for making the dining experience exceptional. The Broccoli Soup, Ragi Kheer, and Gulab Jamuns were genuine highlights of our stay—fresh, perfectly prepared, and consistently delicious..A special mention to Mr. Nandan (Naturalist) as well—his knowledge and guidance added real value to the experience.
Overall, the team did everything one could expect and more. Thank you, Hotel Kumaon, for such a comfortable and memorable stay.
reviews.author: sd md

reviews.text:
The Kumaon is more than a stay—it’s an experience rooted in sustainability, mindfulness, and love for nature. We stayed a night in the garden chalet and 3 nights in the terrace chalet and waking up to those stunning Himalayan views every morning was pure magic!

Everything felt warm and intentional. We requested for vegan food and Chef Naveen went above & beyond. Vegan kumaoni thali with a new dessert in every meal was soul satisfying! Suraj, our resident naturalist organized guided treks to Kasar Devi one day and to the waterfall on another day. We soaked in the beauty of the region and how!  Everyone at the resort was warm and intentional. The culture created by Abhinav (GM) clearly reflects in the staff's day-to-day dealing with the customers!

Last but not the least, a special mention about Juno, Elsa, Coco and Gucci. We loved loved having them around. Juno & Elsa inspired us to take the trip (rescue dogs at the resort are a deal-maker for us always) and finding two more - Coco & Gucci - was icing on the cake!

We loved every moment of our stay at The Kumaon and we will be going back soon!
reviews.author: Tanisha Kashyap

reviews.text:
The Kumaon is a beautiful retreat offering stunning Himalayan views and very comfortable rooms. The Kumaoni home-style food was delicious and added a special touch to our stay.

Property amenities cater perfectly to bird lovers, with multiple small water baths attracting a variety of species, we spotted over 30 + birds right on the premises. What I loved most about this hotel is waking up to birdsong. From orange Minivets to blue whistling thrushes, they greeted me right on the balcony. I even photographed a Himalayan griffon from there.

Day trips to Binsar Wildlife Sanctuary were a highlight.The hotel offers in-house spa facilities, along with good natural paths ideal for trekking and birding.

What truly stood out was the warm, attentive hospitality, with special thanks to Mr. Abhinav for making our stay so memorable.
Overall, it’s a peaceful, relaxing getaway ideal for birding and unwinding in nature.
reviews.author: Rathika Ramasamy

reviews.text: The rooms are amazing; I liked the industrial design, the super comfortable bed, and the fireplace, which I kept on all the time. The place is a bit remote and requires a car. The staff was very helpful and thoughtful of the guests' needs. The food can be better, though
reviews.author: Lobster

reviews.text: Balcony view is amazing. Stay was comfortable and peaceful. Just around 200meter stretch to reach stay is bit tricky, steep walk. Everything else was good, specially the view from balcony. We walked in the Village Sunkiya where the stay is...the village people are very nice.
reviews.author: Barkha Arora

reviews.text: Have been to many places in Himachal and Uttarakhand but this was genuinely one of the best. The location is unbelievably peaceful and quiet. The hosts are amazing and make you feel comfortable without overdoing anything. I never expected the food to be this good. We visited Mukteshwar but ended up having all our meals at the property itself. If you want something offbeat and a different kind of stay, you should check this out. Overall, a solid experience.
reviews.author: priyanshu sharma

reviews.text: This homestay exceeded my expectations. The room was spotless, well-decorated, and very comfortable. The two hosts were genuinely friendly and helpful, offering great local tips and making me feel like part of the family. Highly recommend this place to anyone visiting the area!
reviews.author: Rohit M Baldaniya

reviews.text:
A magical, soul-soothing homestay experience at Saanidhya in Mukteshwar!

Tucked deep in a lush valley of Mukteshwar, surrounded by orchards and farmland owned by a warm local family, Saanidhya is more than just a stay—it’s an experience. Hosts Souniya and Arpit are true locals at heart; their connection to this land is contagious, and they make you feel like family from the moment you arrive.

Every morning, they’d lead us down to a traditional mountain home where Kavita ji and her adorable 6-year-old Krishna welcomed us with open arms. The blue-hued interiors, handmade walls, and cozy fireplace echoed traditions long forgotten, yet alive here. On one adventure, Arpit guided us to a serene riverbed where we dipped in cool waters, plucked potatoes from a farm, and devoured boxes of fried rice he thoughtfully carried for our hungry group. The kindness of the local family—who even cared for our tired daughter and offered her a bed—was deeply touching.

One evening, we were treated to an unforgettable Kumaoni feast cooked on a wood-fired chulha—mooli ki sabji, gheeya, fig chutney, and fresh phulkas served with love. Tea with mishri, evening prayers with the family, and our daughter playing with local kids made it all feel like home.

Lazy afternoons turned into joyful bonding sessions, thanks to Arpit’s knack for bringing guests together with painting, games, and heartfelt conversations. I rediscovered my love for painting, while my husband and daughter played happily. Before we left, Arpit went out of his way to get us crates of fresh figs, plums, and peaches, despite it being the end of the season—a thoughtful gesture we’ll always cherish.

Saanidhya isn’t just a place to stay—it’s a soulful retreat where strangers become friends, traditions come alive, and nature gently slows you down. We left with full hearts, new friendships, and memories we’ll treasure forever. Highly recommend this gem if you want to experience the true essence of Kumaon.
reviews.author: Preeti Sharma

reviews.text: had a very peaceful stay in Mukteshwar. gr8 location and food also is very very good. must go for village walks and local kumaoni experiences hosted by the owners. rooms are big and clean. highly recommend staying in Saanidhya Retreats homestay and not in a hotel.
reviews.author: Himani Pandey

reviews.text:
During my time in Rishikesh, I had the pleasure of attending the Thursday evening Kirtan at Anand Prakash on several occasions. The ashram is beautifully cared for, and the musicians and participants come together to sing and dance in a shared expression of joy, love, and devotion.

I attended regularly as walk-ins are warmly welcomed, without any fee, creating a genuine sense of community and shared wellbeing. I also had the opportunity to join one of Yogrishi Vishvketu's yoga asana classes, where the combination of clear guidance, intention, mantras, and movement made the experience truly unique and deeply memorable.
Thank you for welcoming me so openly.
reviews.author: Allison Fior

reviews.text:
I am so happy that I had the chance to stay at this ashram in Rishikesh. I really enjoyed the daily 5 AM meditations followed by a yoga class. We also had the opportunity to book a stay at their Sri Jungle Ashram, which I highly recommend.

I'm not a big foodie, but the meals were amazing. I am gluten-free, and they kindly made special buckwheat chapatis for me—they were the best!

Regarding the cleanliness of the rooms, it was okay. It could have been better, but it was still acceptable. The location is amazing—within walking distance of Sai Ghat by the river and several temples.

There are also many cute cafes with a great vibe around the area that I highly recommend visiting.

Finally, we had the chance to meet Guru ji, Dr. Yogrishi. He is a very kind and respectful person, and I hope I will have the opportunity to visit him again someday.

Overall, it was a memorable stay, and I would definitely love to go back someday!
reviews.author: Tara Azin

reviews.text: An absolutely marvellous experience! Yoga teacher training was grounded, deeply rooted teachings in traditional yoga, spiritually tuned.  I would have loved a lesson plan for my teacher training experience. Location is fantastic— everywhere is walkable. Cute cafes nearby and an easy walk to Laxman julha
reviews.author: Abha

reviews.text: This was our 1st visit to an ashram and we stayed 4 nights. We followed it up with a week long retreat at another ashram, then another 3 nights at a Rishikesh hotel. Overall, our stay at this ashram was positive. The rooms were nice although the mattresses were thin. The food was good and varied enough so you didn't tire of it. Great chai tea. Lunch and breakfasts were bigger/better than dinner. You sat on  floormats to eat with mini tables. You washed your own dishes. The cleanliness of the floor mats could have been better. They had a beautiful yoga room and the instruction was good, however we never got to see or take a class from the guru. There aren't a lot of props, and the ones they had (mats and bolsters) were in pretty bad shape and were filthy. My suggestion would be to buy a mat when arriving in Rishikesh. The daily fire puja was interesting. The chanting (kirtan) was cancelled both nights we were there. The staff was friendly and there was a pleasant comradary with fellow yogis - when you could talk - quiet time 9 p.m. until after breakfast. Great location.
reviews.author: brian bird

reviews.text: The most beautiful place, so beautifully decorated and with lots of plants, so it feels like a quiet little oasis away from everything. It’s run by the kindest people, who are always around to help you and with such warmth. I stayed for only five days but plan to continue going to their evening classes as I loved it so much. The teachers are great too as is the food!! I hope I can come back to stay again soon!
reviews.author: Cat Stringer

reviews.text:
Tara House the name says it all.
It's truly a home away from home. The kinda food they serve, everyday day the menu keeps changing from Indian to Indo chinese to Mughlai to Tibetan. The mind you the food tastes great.
The services was immaculate. The owners who run the place was super kind and helpful in every given situation.
High speed Wifi was provided too.
All in all the best place to stay in Mudh village.
I was sick for 3 days, had bad cold and cough. Everyday day 3 times they provided me hot water for steam and garlic soup before I sleep without any hesitation. Well that shows the level of hospitality they provide to the guest.
The kid in the picture is Namo, she's the daughter of the owner.
Thank you to Acho and Acha, the owner for an absolute amazing time there.
Ps Nuttela pank cake is must try😅
reviews.author: Jenister Rai

reviews.text: Great location n views from the Tara homestay in mudh village pin valley. Stayed there for 1 night only but truly enjoyed my stay there. The rooms were very comfortable, the hospitality was warm n outstanding and the food was fresh n delicious. Will definitely be back.
reviews.author: ashwini kamath

reviews.text: Very clean and well-maintained room and bathroom. Getting such a top-notch room in such a remote area feels like heaven. The breathtaking natural beauty visible right from the window and balcony is unbelievable. The food was really delicious. The behavior of the owner Sonam Ji’s wife was truly heart-touching.
reviews.author: Sumanta kumar Saha

reviews.text: We spent the night of 21 Oct 2025 at Tara Home Stay. The room was the best of the trip with a heavenly view. This home stay is operating since 2003, and once you stay here, you know why they are thriving now with 3 properties at Mud. It was a truely the warmth and human touch of the homestay that made the stay soo special. Dinner and breakfast we had at their family dinning room with food served, cooked for the family as well as the guests.
reviews.author: Ranadeep Basu

reviews.text:
Tara House Homestay is an excellent place in Mud Village. Although there are limited choices available in this place, the homestay is beyond our expectations. Rooms are spacious, clean and has all the required amenities. The people who run this place are really kind and help the tourists in every possible way. From the moment you land there, you can feel the homely, positive feeling. As soon as arrive, they make sure you get refreshed with tea, coffee and some snacks. In fact, one family which came late the day we landed were shaken by the time they reached there. They did not anticipate the unseasonal snow which covered the entire stretch of the roads and all the mountains around. They reached too late in the night around 8pm and the driver was inexperience too. The owners immediately made some hot maggie and tea for them and assured them that they will take care of them under the extreme weather conditions.
Unfortunately when we went due to power cut due to heavy snow, we had to use only solar powered lights in the room and wifi was not available. This is quite normal in such remote places.
The hosts are really simple and warm people. Food provided by them really tasty and hygienic . Views from the hotel are amazing. I understand the hosts also arrange trekking in the pin valley. The new Tara guesthouse is really place to stay. Highly recommended,
reviews.author: Raghuveera Kamath

reviews.text:
Had such a lovely stay at this beautiful property - The Doksa, nestled right in the middle of the valley. The whole place has a very warm, peaceful and homely vibe that made me feel comfortable almost instantly. It honestly felt like the perfect little escape from the usual rush of life.

What really stood out was how thoughtfully the property has been made. Even though it’s located in such a quaint and remote town, they’ve managed to provide pretty much every amenity and comfort one could think of. The rooms are cozy, clean and extremely comfortable, making it very easy to just relax and enjoy the surroundings, to just be.

The ambience of the place is truly special, waking up to the valley views, the fresh air and the quiet all around was something I genuinely enjoyed every single day.

And the food deserves a big mention. The service was warm and so welcoming, and the food itself was absolutely delicious. There were so many indulgent and elaborate options to choose from, and everything we tried was genuinely tasty and comforting. You can really tell that a lot of care went into curating the overall experience here.

Would definitely recommend this place to anyone looking for a peaceful getaway with great hospitality, amazing food and a stay that just feels easy, comfortable and memorable.
reviews.author: akanksha sharma

reviews.text:
A little off-roading from Mane village takes you into the magical world of Doksa — a truly unique stay tucked away in the offbeat interiors of Spiti. The silence, slow life, and raw beauty here completely mesmerise you.

Anjali, the hostess, brings such wonderful energy and ensures every guest feels comfortable and cared for. The experiences and activities thoughtfully introduce you to the unique lifestyle of Spiti. Despite being far from nearby towns, Mohit and the team make sure the food leaves you amazed every single time.

We spent two wonderful days here and would absolutely love to return again.
reviews.author: Jatin Makkar

reviews.text: A rare find in a rare location. If you have the gumption to withstand an arduous drive and high altitude, then The Doksa is THE place to go to. We happened to be there on its opening day. While there were a few minor technical glitches, I’m sure Mohit — ably supported by the beautiful Property Manager Anjali — will resolve these very soon. The architecture is minimalist (Scandinavian), the rooms are super cosy, and the food is delectable. A must visit for anyone who loves naked mountains, extraordinary terrain and beautiful night skies!
reviews.author: Ashish Sood

reviews.text: It was a wonderful experience staying at the Doksa. Special thanks to Anjali and the staff who looked after us for the time that we were there. Stargazing and capturing the Milky way at night was one of the highlights of the trip. Look forward to going back soon.
reviews.author: Siddharth Rattan

lchang-nang-retreat.editorial: Rustic cottages, a spa & mountain scenery in a laid-back eco-resort along a river.

reviews.text:
We had a wonderful two-night stay at Lchang Nang Retreat with our family, and it was truly a memorable experience.

What stood out the most was the personal attention by the chef. He went out of his way to cater to our specific dietary preference of no onion and no garlic, and even personally visited our cottage to discuss and plan our dinner and breakfast. That level of care made us feel genuinely valued.

The retreat itself is a peaceful haven, surrounded by breathtaking mountains and stunning natural scenery — perfect for anyone looking to disconnect and relax. The heated rooms and heated mattresses ensured complete comfort despite the cold, and the bonfire setup added a lovely touch to our evenings.

Overall, we absolutely loved our stay and would highly recommend it for families looking for a serene and thoughtful getaway.
reviews.author: SDE Chaitanya

reviews.text:
The location is great. Rooms are very nicely done ensuring the usage of sustainable materials.
Food spread is good. However, there is scope for improvement in the service and choice of items in the buffet. For eg, first day there was no starters in the buffet dinner.
Also, deserts options should definitely be increased. Both the days they served kheer, sewayein and rice kheer.
Service is slow at times. But overall a good place to detox.
reviews.author: rajendra jain

reviews.text:
An unforgettable stay at Lchang Nang, Nubra Valley

Our stay at Hotel Lchang Nang was truly a memorable one. The hospitality extended by the staff was exceptional and made us feel completely at home despite the chilling weather.

A special mention to Pervez, Deepak, and Jagjeevan for their outstanding support and warmth — they went above and beyond to ensure our stay was comfortable and hassle-free. Their attentiveness and genuine care to every minute detail made a huge difference.

The location of the hotel was an icing on the cake of our trip. Breathtaking views of snow-capped mountains, which we simply stared at. The food was delicious, and the provision of tea early in the morning with one of the best cookies ever is worth a mention. The room warmers ensured we stayed cozy and comfortable throughout.

Overall, everything—from service and food to comfort and views—was perfect. Highly recommend Lchang Nang to anyone visiting Nubra Valley. Would love to stay here again!

Thank you Rigzin and the entire team at Lchang Nang.
reviews.author: Mohit Totlani

reviews.text:
We stayed at Lchang Nang Retreat, Ladakh for 3 days and booked 3 rooms, and the experience was truly unforgettable. This is a stunning property nestled right in the lap of nature—peaceful, scenic, and incredibly calming. If you’re looking to disconnect from chaos and reconnect with nature, this place is perfect.

The hospitality here deserves special mention. Every staff member and the management team are extremely warm, welcoming, and genuinely helpful. A special thanks to Joydeep (the manager), who went above and beyond for us. He even took us to the Siachen Base Camp, which turned out to be a once-in-a-lifetime experience. We were fortunate enough to see rare things at the base camp and even had the opportunity to have lunch there—something we’ll cherish forever.

The food at the hotel is absolutely delicious, and the way it is served makes the experience even more special. You can truly feel the care and effort put into every meal. On our last day, the team made our stay even more memorable by letting us cook the starter and dinner ourselves. Chef Satish helped us throughout, guided us patiently, and ensured everything was perfectly arranged—it was such a unique and fun experience.

If you love nature, peace, and soulful hospitality, this place is a must-visit. And do not miss having your meals here—it’s an experience in itself. Highly recommended for anyone visiting Ladakh and looking for something truly special. 🌄✨
reviews.author: Rajat Jain

reviews.text:
Our stay at this resort was absolutely fantastic! The rooms were beautifully designed with sustainability in mind and everything from the eco-friendly materials to the energy-efficient systems made our stay comfortable and guilt-free. The staff members were incredibly kind and welcoming, always going out of their way to make us feel at home.

The food was another highlight was fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented. We especially appreciated the complimentary dinner they offered us, which was such a thoughtful gesture and truly made our trip memorable.

Overall, this resort perfectly balances luxury, comfort, and environmental responsibility. I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a relaxing, eco-conscious vacation with exceptional service and amazing food!
reviews.author: Chandini Chandana

reviews.text: I was the only tourist here during the winter. It felt more like a family place and I wanted to stay for 3 days but ended up staying for a week. It was the Ladakhi New Year and I participated in various family celebrations with my hosts. It is very close to the Thiksey Monastery, which was very convenient for morning prayers. I will come back in the summer again.
reviews.author: Le-bing Xie

reviews.text: The host is very cooperative. Room condition, location and the dinner which they have provided are all very good.  Came from the motor boat.  It's a journey to be remembered.
reviews.author: devang pandey

reviews.text:
Horrible state to visit. Manipuri people and police people are not at all welcoming. Their internal. Dispite is killing tourism. And tourist experience.

No Doubt valleys are naturally very beautiful in manipur.
reviews.author: Harsh Vardhan

reviews.text: Beautiful location with beautiful view and host is also very good food was awesome
reviews.author: chirag marwah

reviews.text: Mind-blowing nature peace🧿♥️
reviews.author: RAJAN SAH

reviews.text:
I stayed here for one day in April 2022.
Amazing location, the host was super helpful and very friendly. The Location of this homestay was more than I expected. Its located right in middle of the lake and works on a concept of sustainable. The rooms, toilet and bath room is clean. They provide dinner and breakfast. which was amazing and very tasty.
Any on planning to stay in Loktak. would highly recommend to stay here.
Insta : @traveltalesbyusha
reviews.author: Usha Baliga

reviews.text:
If you are planning a trip to Sikkim, do yourself a huge favor and book at least one night at this beautiful property in Lower Luing. It is located just far enough outside of Gangtok to escape the commercial noise, traffic, and crowds, offering absolute "peace of mind."
​The property itself is gorgeous and incredibly relaxing, but what truly sets it apart is the quality of the people. The hospitality here is wonderful—the hosts treat you with genuine warmth and care, making you feel instantly welcome.
​It is the perfect place to unwind and hit the reset button. Don't just rush through Sikkim; spend at least a night here to relax and experience true local hospitality. I highly recommend it!
reviews.author: Yours Badshah

reviews.text: “Had a wonderful stay at this homestay. The rooms were clean, the food was tasty, and the hosts were very kind and helpful. The location and view were beautiful. Would definitely visit again!”💕
reviews.author: Sonam Ongmu Bhutia

reviews.text: very pretty place with good management, clean spaces, good food and good scenery, enjoyed our  stay and had the best time ever :)
reviews.author: Aashish Chettri

reviews.text: Most wonderful and awesome hotel I have ever seen , literally everything feels amazing and it gives cozy vibes like our home . The best part is food is very tasty like you will never get enough of it .
reviews.author: Trilochana Chettri

reviews.text:
Nestled just a short 20-minute drive from the heart of Gangtok, Luing Village Homestay offers a perfect blend of accessibility and peaceful rural charm. The final stretch adds to the experience—either a quick 1 km off-road drive or a refreshing 10-minute uphill village walk from the main road, giving guests a true taste of local life right from arrival.
What makes this homestay truly special is its unique combination of authentic village living and comfortable luxury. Guests can enjoy beautifully set up glamping tents that provide modern comfort while staying close to nature. The surroundings are serene, quiet, and far removed from the noise of urban life, yet still conveniently located just a few kilometers from Gangtok.
Luing Village Homestay is ideal for travelers seeking a peaceful escape, cultural immersion, and a refreshing connection with nature without compromising on comfort. A perfect getaway for those who want to slow down and experience the simple beauty of village life.
reviews.author: Fly Sikkim Adventure

reviews.text: I stayed at Sha Ri Loum for 2 nights in May 2026 and my experience was nothing short of awesome!! Right from the location, service, cleanliness and hospitality, everything was top notch. The homestay is a very cosy and super clean one with delicious and homely meals served with a lot of love. Merrylyne was always available to cater to all our needs with a smile. The property is very well maintained and makes you feel right at home! This was our best stay in all of Meghalaya….I recommend this place for anyone travelling to Shillong as it is easy to see Shillong’s sights and attractions keeping this homestay as your base. Totally worth every penny!!!😍😍
reviews.author: Sanika Murdeshwar

reviews.text:
This is an excellent home stay with good caretakers and beautiful rooms. We enjoyed our stay here.
In terms of breakfast like others have mentioned, they can certainly improve. Bread jam, and omlette they consider as an item for variety but may not be suitable for everyone especially vegetarians. Apart from this, there is only one variety of cooked breakfast item and is also limited in quantity. I understand you mentioned this is not a hotel but add some fruits at least to offer something to vegetarians.
Dinner they couldn't provide on both days we stayed, so had to order from outside.
reviews.author: Vijay R

reviews.text: Great homestay, spacious rooms, and a warm feeling. Cleaning could be improved; when we stepped in, we found beedi/cigarette butts on the floor. Checkout was late, and it looked like staff members were using the room, as the smell of smoking was still present and the ashtray hadn't been cleaned. The toilet seats need replacement as they are worn out and dirty. Morning breakfast was good. The curtain doesn't close properly and needs to be fixed in the Orchid room. Apart from these hiccups, everything else was good.
reviews.author: arun r

reviews.text:
Very impressive, welcoming, clean home stay . Decor is tasteful WiFi is reliable and the food is delicious !
Farida and her husband are lovely warm hosts , we felt like we were old friends .
Our driver Jordan Crew is know to the hosts well so we would recommend him if you need any transport as he’s a great driver, speaks good English and is very helpful with itinerary’s. Always on time with a smile !
Hope to visit again !
reviews.author: Moona

reviews.text: We had a wonderful stay at Sha Ri Laum Homestay. The food was delicious, homely, and freshly prepared, and the service was excellent throughout our stay. The hosts were very helpful and made us feel comfortable and well taken care of. They also suggested Jayden as our tour guide and driver, and that was one of the best decisions of our trip. Jayden was polite, knowledgeable, and very supportive, which made exploring Meghalaya smooth and enjoyable. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a peaceful stay with great hospitality.
reviews.author: S Singh

sukoon-houseboat.editorial: Elegant inn, occupying a wooden houseboat on Lake Dal, offering a restaurant, a lounge & a sundeck.

reviews.text:
At Sukoon house boat, the ambience is unlike anything a traditional hotel can offer. Waking up to the birds chirping and watching the morning mist rise off the surface of the Dal lake. The interior was beautifully designed — warm wooden accents, cozy lighting, and thoughtful décor that perfectly complemented the natural setting of Srinagar.
The service was exceptional from start to finish. The staff were warm, attentive, and genuinely passionate about making every guest feel at home.
We had our evening tea on the deck listening to the local Kashmiri music performed by the local artists was truly amazing!!
If you’re looking for something beyond the ordinary — a stay that blends luxury, nature, and genuine character — look no further. I will absolutely be returning, and I cannot recommend this experience highly enough.
reviews.author: Mahitha Reddy

reviews.text:
Our stay on the Sakoon houseboat in Srinagar was absolutely wonderful. The setting on Dal Lake is serene and beautiful — the name “Sakoon,” meaning peace, truly fits the experience. The rooms were clean, comfortable, and tastefully decorated, offering both traditional Kashmiri charm and modern comfort.

What truly made our stay exceptional, however, was the hospitality of Waseem and Pervaiz. They went above and beyond to make us feel at home — always attentive, kind, and thoughtful. Whether it was arranging meals, guiding us on local experiences, or simply sharing stories about Kashmiri culture, their warmth and professionalism made all the difference.

We left feeling refreshed and deeply touched by the care we received. I highly recommend Sakoon to anyone visiting Srinagar — and be sure to ask for Waseem and Pervaiz, who will make your stay unforgettable.
reviews.author: Nita Sood

reviews.text:
We stayed for 2 nights and 3 days at Sukoon Houseboat, and it was one of the most refined luxury experiences in Srinagar. The name itself is ‘Sukoon’ — and we truly felt that sukoon throughout our stay.
At first, the pricing may appear slightly higher, but once you experience the quality of the houseboat, the exceptional cleanliness, the beautifully prepared food, and the peaceful terrace overlooking Dal Lake, you immediately realise it is absolutely worth it.

From the start, the speedboat pickup and drop was smooth, safe, and very well maintained. The staff welcomed us warmly and maintained a high standard of hospitality throughout. The interiors blend traditional Kashmiri craftsmanship with modern luxury, creating a calm and elegant ambience.

The complimentary Shikara ride was another highlight. The Shikara was in excellent condition, and both the houseboat staff and the rider shared valuable insights about the Dal Lake floating market, local culture, and the unique lifestyle of the lake community. This added depth and authenticity to our experience.

Overall, Sukoon Houseboat delivers thoughtful service, outstanding cleanliness, true comfort, and a serene luxury atmosphere. Highly recommended for anyone seeking an elegant and memorable stay on Dal Lake.
reviews.author: Pratiek Patre

reviews.text:
Our stay at Sukoon Houseboat was unforgettable, not just because of its serene beauty, but because of the incredible team that made us feel truly cared for from the moment we arrived.

Faizan, the manager, was outstanding. Kind, attentive, and impeccably well-groomed, he ensured every need of ours was met effortlessly. His warmth and professionalism made us feel at home, and his ability to adapt to even the smallest of our requests added immense comfort to our stay.

Waseem, the F&B head, was extraordinary. He went above and beyond to serve us delicious, high-quality meals throughout our stay. Every dish he prepared carried thought, care, and genuine hospitality. Thanks to him, we were constantly well-fed and looked after—he truly made dining on the houseboat a highlight of the experience.

Yasir, who maintained our rooms, deserves a special mention as well. Every day, he ensured our space was spotless, fresh, and well-stocked. We were always hydrated and comfortable because of his consistent, thoughtful service.

And finally, Rashid, our boatman—what a gem. He ensured every journey to and from the houseboat was smooth, convenient, and timely. With him, traveling wasn’t just transport; it became an experience in itself. His skill and calm energy added so much charm to our time in Srinagar.

Overall, the hospitality at Sukoon Houseboat is world-class, delivered by a team that treats guests with genuine care and warmth. We couldn’t have asked for a better stay.
Highly, highly recommended.
reviews.author: prithviraj prasad

reviews.text: Sukoon boathouse is located opp to ghat 19 on dal lake. it is a quite side of dal lake and has a beautiful deck. it is a beautiful boat house. They need to do some up keep like toilets need exhaust, hot water comes after 15 mins of water being wasted to get lake warm water 5 mins after that to get hot water. the bathroom fixtures need changes, toiletries need to be provided as per occupancy, doors are a bit jammed and so are locks. food is good but service is a but lost and slow. the music performance of Santoor performer was the highlight of the stay. Behaviour of the owner on the last hour of my stay was the lowest point. The owner was questioning my booking at the end of our stay. I had booked a one night stay for 3 members with breakfast and lunch or dinner option for 21k. I booked one hour prior to our arrival to the property at 4ish pm. The owner or whoever was sitting at the boathouse reception was rude and was telling me that its not possible to get the deal at this cost. This was a rude behavior, Firstly he had no decency to first ask how was our stay at the property and second he was questioning my voucher of booking as if I got a free stay at his privileged property. I find this kind of entitled behavior very rude. It spoils the mood. The staff is far better behaved. Overall a good property and good food, good staff, avoid talking to the owner or who ever is managing the books. Keep your booking voucher handy, he has a tendency to be rude. The place has good view. Be ready to experience complete quite and peace. I would request the property to make the stay more welcoming as the cost is not low so hospitality needs to be the prime focus.
reviews.author: sabah

reviews.text: We have just spent 7 nights at Coco Shambhala and it was one of the most relaxing resort stays I have ever had.  A quit corner of India to recharge the batteries.  Staff were super friendly, the food was amazing, and a short walk to the beach when I could tear myself away from the villa's infinity pool with the amazing ocean views.  Will definitely be back when I am in India again.
reviews.author: Ian Frentz

reviews.text:
What an experience! We booked all of the 4 villas which really enhances the experience, in my opinion. The villas are incredibly designed, maintained & are very pet/kids friendly.
The food is incredible & do not miss the BBQ! A true pleasure & we’ll be back very soon :)
reviews.author: Stuti Desai

reviews.text: So we had Coco Shamballa in our bucket list for quite some time.. and we had seen numerous images of the Villas on instagram and even more images of Delectable Food ! .. so we thought we knew what to expect...... but what we got back from this break.. was beyond expectations... Yes the Villa Is beautiful.. but you truly have to experience its tropical vibe .. we have spent hours just sitting in the living area staring at the endless darkness in the night ... and yes the food does look good in the images.. but what we got was every meal curated to immaculate perfection.. this was beyond what we expected.. and we have truly never had such lip smacking meals.... but beyond the meals and the villa its the humble hardworking people who make coco what it is..... from arranging cycles for us to ride during our visit.. to arranging anti motion sickness medicines for our scuba diving trip.. the team made sure that we were looked for.. i would highly recommend you to hire cycles and meander across the endless roads and discover temples and beaches. and do go for the nivati fort trek... the nivati beach at the end of the trek is one of the most beaufiful beaches we have visited.. our special mentions to Lumen for fast and efficient bookings , Suhas for taking care of our culinary needs and every whim and fancy... Suhas is an amazing hospitality professional.. with great stories to share.. . a big thank you to Vaibhav our naturalist for patiently accompanying us on our hikes and treks and Amol, dinesh and many more in the service staff who did their best to ensure a comfortable stay.. we will surely be back!!
reviews.author: Conifer Handmades

reviews.text:
Where do I start in describing a most wonderful gathering in Coco's clifftop villas above Bhogwe beach?
A group of 20 of friends came together to celebrate my 60th birthday , travelling from the US, the UK, Denmark, Sweden, Austria, Italy, Tanzania, Ethiopia, India, New Zealand and Australia. I'd booked all 4 villas for three nights and right from the get-go the management and staff at Coco did everything they could to make our gathering an event of a lifetime.

We were awed by the beauty of the Coco Shambhala concept and the panache with which the vision has been executed - a huge hongera sana (congrats in Kiswahli 🙂) to the owner of Coco! We were treated like family - thank you, Revan and all the housekeeping staff for your kindnesses and smiling attention to our various (sometimes troublesome, no doubt) needs.  We dined like kings - Suhas and his sous chefs are such inventive geniuses (pretty much all of us are foodies and the consensus was "I couldn't fault a thing") and we are now testing the recipes they kindly shared on our friends and family (the karripata lemonade - oof!). Rachel handled the reservation process with patience, a sense of humour and sensitive advice - thank you, again.
We were treated to a magnificent bonfire and fireworks display that eclipsed this year's alignment of Mars, Jupiter and Saturn.
We hiked the paths overlooking what must surely be one of the most pristine beaches in India.
We had an unforgettable 'formal' birthday dinner by candlelight and starlight under the Coco cashew tree.
We putt-putted in small boats to the neighbouring Nivati Cove, to explore a new beach where Suhas and his team had a pop-up street-food stall - such a perfect idea!
We lounged by the clear aqua infinity pools in our villas, chatting and reminiscing about our long friendships, hushed by the sunset vistas, and wishing we could stay forever at Coco.
What can I say? If you're thinking of visiting Coco - stop! Don't dream, just go!
Thank you, thank you, thank you all at Coco. I hope to see you again soon.
With love and gratitude,
Criana Connal
reviews.author: Criana Connal

reviews.text: This amazing holiday destination is where we just keep returning, the stunning location with perfect tropical climate must be one of the best in the world. Each villa has beautiful sea views and a fabulous private infinity pool, yet they remain secluded within amazing tropical gardens, teaming with exotic birds. The food has been really exceptional, massages have been so relaxing. The staff couldn't do enough for us, thank you to everyone of you. Our life batteries are now fully charged and we already look forward to seeing you again soon. Noel UK
reviews.author: neil knapton

reviews.text: What an amazing place! Tranquil, right on the beach, great service and great food. The rooms are simple but better than expected considering the price. The staff is so friendly and helpful. We had the best of times at this beach retreat. Would surely come back!
reviews.author: Kakan

reviews.text: We stayed at the new probiertes with 250m walking distance to the beach. Super clean and calm at the property. Beach area is amazing to relax, also pretty clean and calm/peaceful. Restaurant is good and has especially in the evening a very romantic flair. Big recommendation!
reviews.author: Bent Neumann

reviews.text: A lovely spot directly on the beach.  Rooms are comfortable, venue is tidy. We welcomed two nights here. The food service is slow, but hey, slow travel teaches patience and invites you to absorb the wonderful setting. The food is delicious. Well worth the wait. I enjoyed unrolling my yoga mat under palm trees. The sunsets are epic. A lovely vibe.
reviews.author: Yoga with Emma Spencer-Goodier

reviews.text:
Sea Lap Marari Homestay is truly the best place on Marari Beach – it ticks all the boxes.
The beach here is exceptionally clean, which is rare in this area, and perfect for long, peaceful walks by the sea. The atmosphere is incredibly relaxing, exactly what you hope for in Marari.
Guests have sunbeds available, and even if you’re not staying there, they can be rented for about 3 EUR per day, which is great value. The rooms are lovely and literally right on the beach – waking up to the sound of the waves is priceless.
The food is absolutely wow. We enjoyed it so much that we ate at the restaurant every single day, and everything we tried was fresh, delicious, and well prepared.
What really makes the place special is the friendly, attentive staff and the owner, who make you feel genuinely welcome and well looked after without being intrusive.
If you’re looking for a place on Marari Beach with great food, a clean beach, comfortable beachfront rooms, and a calm, authentic vibe, Sea Lap Marari Homestay is the perfect choice. Highly recommended – we would definitely return.
reviews.author: Oana Mogoi

reviews.text: Perfect location on Marari Beach. Hosted by a lovely friendly family, we felt well looked after. The seafood from their restaurant on the beach was some of the best fish we had in Kerala. The rooms were big and clean and we even had a fridge in ours to keep drinks cold in! This was a special place where you can even spot Dolphins whilst relaxing on the beach!
reviews.author: Hannah Walker

reviews.text: it was one of the best experience i had... waking up in the surrounding is heart worming.... located by the riverside.... our whole family enjoyed the peacefull stay .... rooms are very clean and the host is friendly too... restaurant is located few steps away from the stay...it ws the best experience ♥️♥️
reviews.author: Kiran Mulgund

reviews.text:
Amazing location..... Very good experience if uh want peace mode.... sound of nature..
River... loved it...👍
reviews.author: Alisha Lobo

reviews.text: An amazing location with a truly peaceful atmosphere. Surrounded by nature, the soothing sound of the river makes it a wonderful place to relax. Loved the experience.
reviews.author: Abhishek Naik

reviews.text: Such a calm and peaceful place. Waking up to village vibes, a river nearby, and greenery all around was really refreshing. The rooms were modern, clean, and very comfortable. Honestly, the stay felt totally worth the price I paid. Perfect if you want to slow down and relax away from the city.
reviews.author: Pixurious

reviews.text: One of my best experience here along with my friends. I would really love to visit this place again.
reviews.author: 79_Abhishek_Naik

reviews.text:
Our trip was for relaxation at a homely place with home cooked food and this place was just right!
