Peaceful, aesthetically pleasing, green, clean premises very near to the sea beach, no private access currently though, walkable it was. Excellent Tea, breakfast, meals were prepared by staff Setuben with passion and smile. Special requests were readily fulfilled which made our stay very comfortable. The Jain Pooja room with a cupboard full of books added serene vibes to the premises. Chikoo, colourful flowers, betel leaves all make the area very pleasant.
If you need early cup of bed tea, not available in the room but you can get it later. Very good experience, thanks a lot Kusumben for arranging nice stay for us. We enjoyed this first visit and looking forward to more!
reviews.author: Sonal Bakshi

reviews.text: We recently stayed at Omkar home stay and it was a wonderful experience. The room was big and tidy, location was very close to the beach with a private access, premises were full of green trees, and food was tasty and homelike. We appreciate the food related special requests which were graciously fulfilled. Highly recommended for a peaceful, homelike stay. Thank you Kusumben.
reviews.author: Naimish

reviews.text: This property in Bordi is excellent, with Bordi Beach just a short walk away. It offers three rooms, a hall, and a kitchen for rent. The kitchen is exceptionally clean, and helpers are available to assist you. If you prefer to cook yourself, you're welcome to do so. This is an ideal place for families looking to experience the pristine beaches of Bordi and Dahanu.
reviews.author: Sagar Bhalerao

reviews.text: The experience of staying here and having their homely cooked food was awesome. The owners and other helpers were very polite. The rooms, garden and the backyard are extremely clean. Bordi beach is accessible even from the back gate of the property. Lots of Chickoo trees on the premises, adds to the pleasure. Would love to visit again
reviews.author: Deepak Pandit

reviews.text:
The rooms are clean and spacious. The layout of the house is also very good. The food is prepared by the owner , Kusum bhabhi, and you get good homely, tasty food.
Our dog was also quite relaxed. We had a relaxing weekend.
reviews.author: Rajas Jukar

reviews.text:
On 10th December 2025 i reached the RIVER HOUSE property! Before reaching there i was a bit anxious as to how it would be. But when saw the premises i was mesmerized! One the best accomodations in which i have stsyed so far. Awesome, beautiful, superb and what not. I am falling short of the adjectives.
To add to it excellent service, very yummy food for dinner as well as the breakfast. Excellent is the word applicable.
I was there barrly for 21 hours but till now regretting that i got very short time there. Would love to visit again.
All credit to the owners of the property and the dedicated staff.
Rooms: 5/5  |  Service:
reviews.author: sanjeev modak

reviews.text: A stunningly beautiful, enormous house with only five guest rooms all very spaced out. A glorious walkway through lush gardens full of flowers and spice trees to a  refreshing pool and a covered, but glorious open air restaurant that overlooks the river. Lovely, comfortable, spacious and simply appointed room. We were lucky enough to be hosted by the delightful Dr. Simon who along with his lovely wife Molly, created this resort (and the Windemere Estate, also outstanding).  The food was fabulous, made from the best of the day, every meal was a feast with the wonderful chef very happy to cater for different diets/tastes.  Standout staff   - though all were charming - were Prakash and Tony. We also had a few great early morning yoga sessions with Rosilyn who introduced us to a very different way of practice which we really enjoyed. Highly recommend!
reviews.author: Eileen Wada Willett

reviews.text: My second visit to this very fine property and was again so impressed with the staff, the food and the experiences. The morning village walk/ birdwatching is a must as well as the amazing therapeutic yoga sessions. Rosily was an amazing instructor- full of wisdom and great information.
reviews.author: nancy koeppel

reviews.text:
A Hidden Gem by the River – Truly Unforgettable Stay!
Windemere Riverhouse is an absolute paradise! From the moment we arrived, we were welcomed with such warmth and genuine hospitality that made us feel right at home. The property is stunningly located by the serene river, surrounded by lush greenery, a perfect place to unwind and soak in the beauty of Kerala.
Our room was spacious, spotless, and tastefully designed with a lovely river view. Every detail, from the cozy interiors to the thoughtful amenities, reflected great care and attention. The food was delicious, freshly prepared with authentic Kerala flavours, and served with so much love.
What truly makes Windemere Riverhouse special are the people behind it. The hosts and staff go above and beyond to make your stay memorable. Their kindness, local insights, and personal touches make you feel more like a guest in a friend’s home than in a hotel.
We spent our mornings watching the river come alive and our evenings enjoying peaceful sunsets, an experience we’ll always cherish. Among all the places we stayed in Kerala, this one truly stood out for its tranquility, beauty, and heartwarming hospitality.
Highly recommended for anyone looking for an authentic, peaceful, and soul-refreshing stay in Kerala. We can’t wait to return!
reviews.author: Uttam Pradhan

reviews.text:
A beautiful hotel, actually it’s not a hotel but a home and I can’t think of enough superlatives. Imagine your idea of paradise with a lovely pool, the sound of birds and lovely tropical gardens and this is it. The food was fantastic and Tony who was our waiter is brilliant 🤩. John, who is the manager/owner could not do enough for us. Calm, knowledgeable and very helpful and kind. We had 2 yoga sessions with Rosilyn who runs a local yoga centre. She was amazing - we’re converted !
Must also take the morning or/and afternoon bird walks with Dinesh (apologies if the spelling is incorrect). He’s such a nice man, so engaging and a brilliant bird spotter!
reviews.author: Jo Hatton

reviews.text: Longwa (Lungwa) is a unique village in Nagaland located on the India–Myanmar border, with the international boundary passing right through the village. It’s a beautiful place with warm and welcoming people. The Konyak King’s Palace is the most unique and fascinating part of the village, reflecting the rich heritage and culture of the Konyak tribe.
reviews.author: Gauranga K Khataniar

reviews.text: Longwa is a unique village located in the Mon district of Nagaland, India, that straddles the international border with Myanmar. Its most notable features are that the village lies in two countries, the chief's house is bisected by the border, and its residents hold dual citizenship. The village is the home of the Konyak tribe, known for their rich cultural history.
reviews.author: Hironmoy Gogoi

reviews.text: Very unique place, must hire a guide they'll tell you fascinating things about Longwa, Konyak tribe, king and this place itself. Indo-mynmar border goes through this palace. And you can buy locally made souvenirs and earrings etc stuff too.
reviews.author: Ghanshyam Salunke

reviews.text:
The people of longwa was kind hearted.
Its a must visit place and we can also see the india Myanmar border.
And the king of longwa was a really nice guy🤍💯
reviews.author: Aashiq Muhammed

reviews.text: A different type of house or palace which is not commonly seen elsewhere. Dressed with do many heads of different animals including humans is a total different in a house. Other things are the horns of deers and heads of Mithun cow is a notable gmfactor. The story of King is a fairy tale and fear producing. Yet I enjoyed this diverse tourism.

reviews.text: I recently spent a night in Khonoma village in Nagaland, and the experience was nothing short of enchanting. Khonoma beautifully blends age-old traditions with subtle modern comforts, creating a vibe that feels both rooted and refreshing. The village is incredibly clean, peaceful, and surrounded by breathtaking greenery. What struck me most was the warmth of the people—every interaction felt genuine, welcoming, and proud of their heritage. Walking through the terraced fields, exploring the traditional homes, and learning about the village’s conservation history made the visit truly memorable. At the same time, the modern touches in homestays and local amenities made the stay comfortable and convenient without taking away from the authenticity. Khonoma is a perfect escape for anyone looking to slow down, connect with nature, and experience a culture that’s preserved with love. I’m already looking forward to returning!
reviews.author: Amit K. Chaudhary

reviews.text:
Khonoma village is 20 km away from Kohima the capital city of Nagaland. It is a quiet  village overlooking the mesmerizing terraced paddy fields and hills covered with lush forests. Often described as a ‘warrior village’, Khonoma is known for its fierce resistance during the British colonial period. It is a historical village of Nagaland. .Khonoma is also the first Green Village in Asia. With its naturally blessed resources, intriguing history and rich culture, the village is one of the most visited places in Nagaland.
Just near the car parking there is an office where you will get a  guide who will show you the quaint village . Local guide is a must. Without a guide one can not explore Khonoma's beautiful narrow lanes . As the trail starts you will see some long bamboo sticks and umbrellas kept for the tourist to use. If you wish you can pay  10 rupees for using the stick or you can use it without paying. When you finish the trail just put the stick back in its place. It is advisable to take a stick and an umbrella as you will have to climb up many stone steps with the guide and umbrella is needed for the sun. One has to pay a small amount for using the umbrella. As you climb up ,you will find some wooden shelves by the stone steps where you will find locally made juice packed in polythene packets with a straw , you will also find varieties of local fruits.Prices of juice and fruits are written in papers . In one corner of the shelf you will see a box where it is written "pay here". It is actually a shop without a shop keeper. Tourists  take whatever they want and put the total amount of money in the box. Our guide, a village youth was polite and gave us time to relax and enjoy the beautiful views. He also gave all the answers to our quarries ,told us Khonoma's history very interestingly .We listened the stories that never made it to history books. Our guide also shared stories of tribal customs, clan traditions and architecture .
He took us to every nook and corner to show the beautiful village , everywhere there were flowers and greenery., he showed us the traditional angami houses made of wood and bamboo  each with symbolic animal skulls hanging above the doors ,a remnant of their hunter past.
At every turn,we found there’s a story of war, courage or conservation. He showed us the big long Guns which are preserved in their traditional youth hostel. We found a couple of small shops of traditional bags , mufflers, scarves bamboo baskets etc. We bought beautiful hand woven angami scarves . On our way we also found a tiny library.We found many home stays in Khonoma all are overlooking the mesmerizing terraced paddy field and the hills. After we finished our walk , our guide took us to  a nice little wooden cafe where we found awesome tribal ornaments . There is an interesting nook in the cafe , which can be called a window nook . There is a wooden table and a chair in the nook under a window . You just sit there , through the window you will see the endless green terraces step gracefully down the hills, glowing gold in the sun, framed by forested hills, a pure poetry in motion. I am sure a true nature lover ,a poet or a writer can sit here for the whole day gazing to the pure poetry in front. The owner of the cafe while preparing coffee and maggie for us informed us that one can trek through the lush forest trails and spot rare birds like Blyth’s Tragopan, or can simply soak in the serenity of untouched wilderness. It is a paradise for photographers  and nature lovers . With its naturally blessed resources, intriguing history and rich culture, this village is one of the most visited places in Nagaland. Our visit to Khonoma was worthwhile.
reviews.author: bobita saikia

reviews.text:

suryauday-haveli-varanasi.editorial: Elegant rooms, some with river views, in a venue offering a vegetarian restaurant.

reviews.text:
Varanasi is one of these spiritual locations where you should not expecting a 5-star hotel. But I give this a high rating because of the location, history of the hotel, excellent service and great breakfast buffet (small but mighty).

Things to note:
Lots of small alleyways in Varanasi which means getting to the hotel involves walking about 5 minutes in these to get to the hotel. (Thank goodness for the staff schlepping our luggage!)
Lots of steps to get to the river (out of the hotel and then out to the water).
Room WiFi slow but go out to the patio area!
Make use of the high tea!  Both days had something different and again, small but interesting selection.
reviews.author: Judy Leach

reviews.text:
We spent 3 nights at this hotel. It’s an old palace that belonged to the king of Nepal that’s converted into a hotel. Only has 14 rooms. The price we paid was a bomb… more than a Taj in Mumbai. For that I would rate the rooms a 3. Very small and cramped. We had a dinner here one night. The live music was nice but the food was cold and very average. Nothing stood out. The service was attentive & courteous but we didn’t eat there again. Also the breakfast buffet laid on the terrace with a beautiful view of the ghats .. only had the view , again the food was cold & uninspiring. Pity because Banaras is known for its lip smacking food, but the chefs here need immediate training . Also it’s very hard to get to the hotel… not recommended for older guests or people who find it hard to walk.
Sorry to say, other than the view this hotel has nothing else to speak for it . I would find an alternate place next time I visit Varanasi

reviews.text: The service at Amritara Suryauday Haveli is where the property truly shines, transforming a great stay into an unforgettable one. From the moment you arrive, the staff treats you with a level of warmth and "Atithi Devo Bhava" (the guest is God) hospitality that feels genuinely personal.                                                                            "A special mention must go to our guide, Mr. Vishal, who looked after us exceptionally well. His deep knowledge of the city's history and his ability to navigate the vibrant chaos of the ghats made our experience seamless and deeply enriching. He was professional, patient, and went out of his way to ensure we felt comfortable and well-informed throughout our tours. If you stay here, do ask for him—he truly elevates the entire experience!"

reviews.text:
Overall:
•	Poor value for money. Not recommended, especially for elderly travelers or anyone with mobility issues.

Pros:
•	The terrace view is beautiful.
•	Staff are very warm and courteous and genuinely try to help. This review is not a reflection of their attitude, which was excellent.

Cons:
•	Extremely difficult access to the hotel.The final stretch is through dark, uneven lanes where no vehicle can enter.I was traveling with my 70+ year old parents who require wheelchair assistance at airports, and reaching the hotel was extremely difficult. While the staff helped with wherl chair everytine we came out - just the verry bumpy ride in wheel chair thru the street made our parents not venture out
•	One of them almost fainted from exhaustion after walking through the lane late at night.Impossible to reach the hotel by phone.I tried calling the property multiple times before arrival using the number on Google and their website..Even Cleartrip said they couldn’t reach the property, which was surprising.
•	Breakfast is extremely poor.Very limited spread and mediocre quality.
•	For a hotel costing ₹20,000+ per night, this was one of the worst breakfast experiences I’ve had.
•	Value for money is very poor given the price and overall experience.Management response was underwhelming.After explaining the difficulty my elderly parents faced, the only compensation offered was “lunch on us.”
•	Side note: Losing the room key costs ₹1000, so keep it safe.

Summary:
•	Lovely staff and nice terrace view, but poor accessibility, weak management, and very poor value for money.
•	Fully avoid if traveling with elderly people or anyone with mobility issues.
reviews.author: MK Balaji

reviews.text:
We had a really lovely stay at this resort. The property is peaceful, well maintained, and perfect if you’re looking to relax and switch off for a bit. The rooms were clean and comfortable, and the overall vibe of the place felt very welcoming.
A special mention to Chef Pawan, super friendly, warm, and genuinely passionate about what he does. He went out of his way to make sure the food suited our taste and was always happy to chat. The meals were fresh, tasty, and felt thoughtfully prepared.
Overall, a great experience with kind staff and good hospitality. Would definitely recommend this resort for a calm and comfortable getaway, and would happily stay here again.
reviews.author: Shubhra Motgill

reviews.text:
My stay at Sea Shells Agatti was truly unforgettable. Located on the pristine shores of Agatti Island, this resort offers a perfect blend of natural beauty and comfort. The turquoise lagoon right in front of the property feels like a private paradise — crystal-clear water, white sandy beach, and breathtaking sunsets every evening.
The rooms are spacious, clean, and thoughtfully designed with a tropical touch. Waking up to the sound of waves and stepping directly onto the beach is an experience in itself. The housekeeping and overall maintenance were excellent throughout our stay.
The staff deserves special appreciation — extremely courteous, helpful, and always ready to assist with a smile. From airport transfers to arranging water sports and island excursions, everything was handled smoothly and professionally.
The food was another highlight. Fresh seafood, delicious local Lakshadweep cuisine, and well-prepared Indian dishes made every meal enjoyable. The dining area overlooking the lagoon adds to the overall charm.
Water activities like snorkeling, kayaking, and scuba diving were well organized, and the marine life around Agatti is simply mesmerizing.
Overall, Sea Shells Agatti is a perfect destination for those seeking peace, natural beauty, and warm hospitality. Highly recommended for couples, families, or anyone looking to unwind in a tropical paradise. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

reviews.text:
One of the most underrated bird watching sites that attracts 11000 different species of birds migration from Siberia. This wetland around Chilika lake is a paradise for both domestic and migratory birds that calls it home between October and late February.
The wetland can be accessed through manual boats with a guide that has comprehensive knowledge on the species and their names and the history.
Our guide Sygyana was fantastic and needs a special mention.
The place is maintained by poachers turned to conservationist.
Accessible by road for a 2 hr drive from puri or bhubaneswar.
The boat ride for upto a 4 or 5 costs 1500 rupees along with a guide.
Better to prebook the trip by googling Mangalajodi eco tourism.

reviews.text:
What an amazing place to spent in the midst of nature. Winters is a very favourable time to visit here. I was here with parents, who loved the food, vibe and the guys here are so adjusting and politely take care of all the needs.

We went boating in the afternoon 3 -5:30 pm which was great which is included in the package.
The charges include lunch+ high tea+ dinner + breakfast +one time boating.

reviews.text:

reviews.text:
I recently visited Kisama Heritage Village, Kohima, Nagaland during the Hornbill Festival, and it was an unforgettable cultural experience. Kisama truly reflects the soul of Naga traditions, showcasing rich heritage, vibrant customs, and the unity of different tribes in one place.

The festival atmosphere was energetic and colorful, with traditional dances, folk music, indigenous games, local crafts, and authentic Naga cuisine. Every corner of the village felt alive with history and culture, making it a perfect destination for anyone interested in Northeast India’s traditions.

I stayed in a tent accommodation within the festival area, which cost ₹900 per person for one night. The tents were simple yet comfortable, well-organized, and suitable for travelers who want to stay close to the festival activities. Staying there made it easy to enjoy late-night cultural performances and early morning events without any hassle.

The local people were extremely warm, welcoming, and proud of their culture. Their hospitality added great value to the overall experience. The surroundings were clean, scenic, and well-managed despite the large number of visitors.

Overall, Kisama Village during the Hornbill Festival is a must-visit destination for cultural explorers, photographers, and travelers seeking authentic experiences. It beautifully blends tradition, community spirit, and celebration, leaving visitors with lasting memories.
reviews.author: Raktimraj Gogoi

reviews.text:
It was my first visit of Nort East, but definitely not last. Beautiful people, amazing culture.
I was lucky enough to visit this place during an fantastic festival Hornbill in 2025. I was amazed how well everything was organized, how beautifully designed this place (the cherry blossoms added a special touch)
This is a unique opportunity to observe the traditions and culture of the Naga tribes. Very authentic, very colorful, a complete delight. I would definitely recommend to all my friends who have a desire to travel around India and get acquainted with its culture.
reviews.author: Aleksadra Gaben

reviews.text: During the hornbill festival, Kisama heritage village if filled with different cultural gatherings of naga tribes and it is a very pleasing experience to be a part of it. If anyone is planning to visit then 1-10 december is very nice and try to spend the night there to get a better experience of the festival
reviews.author: Manash Pratim Deka

reviews.text:
📍 Kisama Heritage Village – Kohima, Nagaland

✨ Why people love this place: • A wonderful place to experience Naga tribal culture, traditions, and architecture in one location. You can see traditional huts (morungs) of different Naga tribes and learn about their rich heritage.
• Surrounded by peaceful hills and scenic views, making it a calm and relaxing place to visit.
• During the Hornbill Festival, Kisama comes alive with colorful cultural performances, traditional music & dance, delicious local food stalls, and beautiful handicrafts.
• Warm and friendly locals add to the charm, and visitors can enjoy authentic Naga cuisine and shop for local crafts.
🔎 Things to know before visiting: • Outside the Hornbill Festival season, the village is quieter with fewer activities.
• Some visitors feel that more year-round cultural events, food stalls, and art displays would make the visit even better.
📌 Final words: Kisama Heritage Village is a must-visit for anyone who wants to understand the soul of Nagaland. It’s vibrant and unforgettable during the Hornbill Festival, but even on peaceful days, it offers cultural learning, beautiful surroundings, and a soothing atmosphere.
🌿 Perfect for culture lovers, photographers, and anyone looking for a calm and meaningful travel experience.
reviews.author: Humesh Dayalam

reviews.text: It's such a beautiful tourist spot , you can get to understand the variety of culture, their way of life and how they lived and living. Very beautifully done up , each village representing a tribe. I was awe struck the way they have exhibited everything.

reviews.text: A vacation at Kolukkumalai Tea Estate feels like stepping into a quiet dream perched above the clouds, where time slows down and nature takes the lead. As the world’s highest tea plantation, it offers sweeping views of mist-covered valleys, crisp mountain air, and endless stretches of emerald tea bushes that glow softly at sunrise. Mornings begin with the gentle hum of the wind and a cup of freshly brewed tea, rich and aromatic, made from leaves plucked just steps away. Wandering through the estate, watching traditional tea-making methods, and soaking in the serene silence create a deep sense of calm. It’s a place where simplicity becomes luxurious, and every moment feels refreshingly pure and unforgettable. 🍃☕
reviews.author: Samuel Joseph Raj

reviews.text: Superb location... Maybe not suitable for aged people.. Really rough offroad drive.. Perfect for thrill seekers.. Will visit again definitely.. Bcoz the view was worth it at the top.. Heavenly

If we intrested stay here we can. Many travellers and tourists visiting here for trekking. We can see very beautiful sunrise here. Here have a beautiful view point. Strongly recommends.

reviews.text: Stunning views, frustrating journey. While the vistas from Bose Peak and Kolukamalai viewpoint are incredible, the dangerous 30-minute off-road drive to reach them is a major drawback - especially at such a high price point. The path urgently needs grading for basic safety. Furthermore, the air pollution from unchecked diesel vehicles undermines the natural splendor. A beautiful destination in need of responsible management.

the-bangala.editorial: Tranquil hotel with an open-air restaurant & an outdoor pool, plus breakfast.

reviews.text:
Had a wonderful stay at The Bangala. The hospitality was excellent, staff were friendly, humble, and always ready to help. The rooms were spacious, and the entire place has a beautiful traditional feel.

They took the time to explain their culture and customs, and even gave a guided walk through the mansion, which was very informative. The food was authentic and delicious, especially the orthodox sets of home made dishes. Overall, a memorable stay, would definitely visit again!
reviews.author: BixBi

reviews.text:
The Bangla appears to he catering to more of Non Indian guests. It is an old structure which has been expanded with new rooms and amenities.
However at Rs 12,000/night for double occupancy, this small 33 room hotel pegs itself, as a premium offering. We found the place poorly managed. For its price, I would have expected much better management and services. A simple thing like getting bed tea, is a slightly complex process.
Being located bang on a busy highway whithin the town, the place is noisy. Staff processes are not in place. Toiletries were extremely basic. Even basic moisturisers and conditioners are not available.
On the positive side, quality of linen in the rooms was excellent. The Athamgudi tiles on the floor add to the heritage feel of the place. Multicoloured bougainvillea in full bloom, added to the cha if the place. Our 1st floor room overlooking the small pool had a nice view. The common verandah seating was nice.
reviews.author: Rajeev Gupta

reviews.text: Had a wonderful experience at this authentic Karaikudi-style property. The ambiance is calm, traditional, and very pleasing. The service was excellent and made the stay comfortable. The food is a bit on the expensive side, but the taste and quality make it worth trying. Overall, a highly recommended place for anyone looking for a cultural and relaxing stay.

reviews.text:

The air-conditioning works well and the seating arrangement can accommodate a large number of guests comfortably. The dining area is also spacious and well maintained, allowing guests to have their meals without much crowding. Another big advantage is the ample parking space, which is very helpful for large gatherings.

reviews.text: A lovely property, tastefully decorated with excellent room amenities. It’s a nice getaway place for a couple of nights. Not too much to do outside so you can enjoy the property. The food was delicious, authentic Chettinad cuisine. Overall, service was excellent.

visalam-chettinad.editorial: Polished hotel in a converted mansion featuring dining, a pool & traditional Tamil games & dance.

reviews.text:
We stayed at Visalam during our vacation for two days, and the experience was truly wonderful. The hospitality, ambience, and food were all outstanding and exceeded our expectations.
A special mention to Chef Vivekananda, who was extremely kind and thoughtful. We had casually spoken about crab masala the previous night, and he went the extra mile to prepare it specially for us the next day. I must say, it was the best crab masala I have ever tasted.
All the food served had authentic Chettinad flavors, tasting just like home-cooked meals—no artificial flavors, no excessive oil, just pure and traditional cuisine done right.
Overall, Visalam offers a warm, memorable stay with exceptional food and service. Highly recommended for anyone looking for an authentic and peaceful experience.
reviews.author: Siji Raju

reviews.text:
Our stay at Visalam by CGH Earth during our Chettinad visit was truly memorable. From the moment we arrived, we were welcomed with genuine warmth and hospitality that made us feel right at home.

A special mention to Yoga, who was incredibly helpful throughout our stay. Their attentiveness and willingness to go the extra mile really added to our overall experience.

The property itself is stunning — beautifully preserving its heritage while offering a peaceful and comfortable atmosphere.

We thoroughly enjoyed the services, the ambiance, and the entire experience. It’s a place that leaves a lasting impression, and we are definitely looking forward to visiting again.
reviews.author: AMAL SEBASTIAN

reviews.text: Visalam is a class-act in every respect. The property itself is absolutely gorgeous and represents the fine mansions of Chettinad beautifully. The manager and the staff are outstanding, attending to guests’ every need with charm, humour and grace. The food was fabulous and was a great introduction to the Chettinad cuisine. We loved our stay here and were sorry that our two days were over too quickly. We’d love to come back again soon!
