reviews.text:
We had an extremely wonderful experience at CGH Visalam when I visited a few months ago. The warmth, hospitality, and attention to detail—everything was perfect at Visalam. I had a great Chettinad cuisine class; special thanks to Letchmi Akka for that unique experience. Kavitha Akka, who is from Kanadukathan, showed me her beautiful village with so much pride.
The way they showcase their heritage village is truly appreciable. Thank you once again for the wonderful experience.
reviews.author: Kaveri Nair

reviews.text:
Visalam CGH is a beautiful property to stay, stayed 2 nights with a group of travellers and was a memorable one.
My room was a heritage room with poster bed and balcony, very spacious and very clean and tidy. This particular room has the bed on a height and a small stool is required to step on to the bed, so before booking plz check with reception if you are a senior citizen, have small kids or knee problem.
Food was delicious but not sure due to Navrati the dishes we less. My favorite was the filter coffee at the coffee cart, the girl was very attentive to our order and served everyone the perfect drink.
Evening walk is a must, as Mr. Shiva has many interesting stories to tell.
We girl had an amazing time around the mansion.
Cooking classes was very educational and loved the dish made, we all got to try it in the end.
Amazing professional staff and will surely visit again.
Only downfall was the spa, Massage was not up to the mark for the price.
reviews.author: Mallika Jamy

villa-retreat-kodaikanal.editorial: Relaxed cottages in a hotel offering gardens, a casual restaurant & breakfast.

reviews.text:
I recently had the pleasure of staying at Villa Retreat, and I must say it was a truly wonderful experience! The ambiance was serene, and the hospitality was top-notch.

A huge shoutout to the restaurant staff - Amzath Khan, Shankar, and Chitra - for their amazing service and the Reception Staff Buvaneswari! They were attentive, friendly, and made sure we had everything we needed. Their warm smiles and willingness to help made our stay even more enjoyable.

The complimentary buffet spread was huge & delicious, and the overall experience was relaxing and rejuvenating.

Kudos to the team, and thank you for making our stay so memorable.

Highly recommended.
reviews.author: Kousic M

reviews.text:
I recently stayed for four nights with my family in the Perumal Malai View Room during mid-May, and overall, the experience was pleasant with a few areas that could be improved.

Pros: The biggest highlight of the property is undoubtedly its serene location and beautiful valley views, which can be enjoyed both from the restaurant’s front yard and the room balcony. The breakfast buffet was impressive, especially the traditional South Indian dishes, complemented by a few à la carte options. Another major advantage is the hotel’s excellent location — Coaker's Walk and Bryant Park are situated very close to the entrance, while Kodaikanal Lake and the local market are within walking distance. The hospitality and courteous nature of the hotel staff also deserve special appreciation.

Cons: On the other hand, the rooms across categories appear slightly overpriced considering the overall facilities offered. The food quality during regular meals was average compared to the excellent breakfast experience. Although the property enjoys a prime location, it can get noisy at times due to its proximity to the adjacent road, open parking area near Kodai Resort, early morning sounds over the valley from nearby churches & mosque and the constant movement of visitors heading to Coaker’s Walk. Additionally, while the cool weather in Kodaikanal may explain the absence of air conditioning, the rooms also do not have ceiling fans, which some guests may still prefer for ventilation at low speed. It would also be helpful for the hotel to consider providing room heaters during the colder winter & rainy season.

Overall, the stay was enjoyable, mainly because of the scenic location, accessibility, and warm hospitality. However, there is definitely scope for improvement in terms of room amenities, pricing, and noise management to enhance the overall guest experience further.
reviews.author: Dinesh Hattarki

reviews.text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Wonderful Stay – Felt Like Home with Stunning Mountain Views

We had a truly wonderful experience staying at Villa Retreat. From the moment we arrived, we felt very welcomed, and the hospitality made us feel completely at home.

The location is excellent, close to the main attractions yet peaceful and calm. The highlight of our stay was the breathtaking mountain views — waking up to misty hills and fresh air was simply magical.

The rooms were clean, comfortable, and well maintained, and the overall ambience of the property is warm and homely. The staff were courteous, attentive, and always ready to help, which made our stay even more pleasant.

Villa Retreat is perfect for couples and families looking for a cozy stay with great views, comfort, and a peaceful atmosphere. We thoroughly enjoyed our time here and would definitely love to stay again.

Highly recommended for anyone visiting Kodaikanal!
reviews.author: Hemanth Kumar Kurreli

reviews.text:
Wouldn’t recommend staying here. The Staff is very helpful. The food is good as well. But the room had a very damp smell. They aren’t being maintained well. It was a struggle to stay put in room for an entire night. The management knew about it yet they are leasing the room, which seemed really unfair.
The manager on duty was kind enough to refund our money for the remaining 2 days.  Yet it doesn’t change the fact that my mother and I had to really struggle to breathe through the night in their accommodation which is 3 star accommodation and priced in the 6k plus category per night.
reviews.author: Beera Hema

reviews.text:
Hi,
We are happy so much about  stay in our resort . Pleasant and peaceful stay very near kodai city main busstand. highlight is very good ambience, garden, cleanliness room and care.wooden rooms makes  suite luxury and warm.our room window view is much loveable one everytime view the misty hills.More than worth for money.We had pleasant stay,nice food and great happiness.thanks to  Villa Retreat  manager and staffs those who cares us .
reviews.author: Bharath singh

sadhana-forest-auroville.editorial: Non-profit center geared to reforestation, water conservation & food management, open to volunteers.

reviews.text:
I visited this place based on Google reviews, mainly out of curiosity. It is well established for its purpose and is designed for both short and long stays
guests can stay for a week or even longer. The experience is centered around living close to nature, with a strong focus on sustainability and eco-friendly practices, so visitors should come prepared for that kind of environment. There are dormitories for individuals as well as separate accommodations for families. The food is prepared on site and is entirely vegan, and the property also includes a cattle farm. Overall, it offers a unique and meaningful experience, though it may not be everyone’s cup of tea. For casual visitors, spending about two hours here is more than sufficient.
reviews.author: Vinay Kumar

reviews.text: I did the Friday tour and I enjoyed so much learning about not just how the community plants trees, but created an ecosystem, leads a zero-waste & vegan lifestyle, cares for cows, cooks their food, shares activities together and provides an alternative schooling environment for children. We shared a most delicious vegan meal together and watched a fascinating documentary. This is a living permaculture project that beautifully and successfully demonstrates the gifting economy and compassionate co-existence ♥️
reviews.author: Nayla Yehia

reviews.text: We visited there as a visitor just out of curiosity. They explained everything patiently and showed us around. Since we visited during evening time, it's dinner time around there so they provided us with good vegan food. We enjoyed the new experience and got amazed truly. Planning to stay more to get more experience on the natural living.
reviews.author: Monisa Ramesh

reviews.text:
Amazing event, felt like an extended family. The space and people are so welcoming and warm. Workshops activities speeches discussion form were engaging and informative. If you are planning to be a vegan, it's the place you must spend time with likewise community.
Thanks Sadhana Forest Festival organizers for such a wonderful experience...
reviews.author: Anjula Awasthi

reviews.text:
I have found my 'Home away from home'. That's what Sadhana forest is for me. It has bring the best out of me.
From the looks of it, it's a re-forestation program and a community living in the forest and doing so very sustainably. But what really it does is more than that. It grows people into love and compassion. The happiness that you have been always chasing in life but never really attaining it, that's what you find yourself in here - the true bliss. It's a safe space where you really can be your true self. I found myself in the 2nd week of staying here itself. Because of all the love showered on me everyday, people here really know how give without expectations.

Must visit to experience life in it's truest beauty.
reviews.author: Piyush Vishwakarma

harivihar-heritage-homestead.editorial: Relaxed rooms in a serene hotel offering free Wi-Fi & a garden, plus yoga & Ayurvedic treatments.

reviews.text:
We did a 21-day Panchakarma ayurvedic detox retreat at Harivihar.
The Harivihar family, the staff, was always there to support us, answer our questions, and recommend activities for us, in addition to being very friendly and attentive to our comfort. The Ayurvedic food served was delicious. Our beautiful, spacious room, that we sometimes shared with mosquitoes (the staff was proactive about it), was charming and comfortable. In terms of detox treatment, daily meetings with our doctor provided us with a wealth of information about our health and the main principles of Ayurveda, and we had the opportunity to do several blood tests that are not as easy to obtain in our home country. The treatments, although demanding on some days (such is the nature of Panchakarma), allowed us to experience Ayurveda and enjoy four-hand massages most days (abhyanga). We hope to meet again !
reviews.author: Nali T Paradis

reviews.text:
Harivihar is such a beautiful place, with a green garden away from the uproar of the city. I stayed there a week beg of february and enjoyed every part of it   : comfortable accomodation, great ayurvedic food, very professional ayurvedic doctor & massage.
The host and the team are welcoming you with smile and make you feel home. I had such a wonderful retreat there !
reviews.author: Sylvie Megret

reviews.text:
Our stay at Harivihar Heritage Retreat in Kozhikode was truly superb, offering an extraordinary blend of historical authenticity and modern luxury. This meticulously restored  ancestral home built by the erstwhile  Kadathanad royal family serves as more than accommodation—it's a living museum of Kerala's architectural heritage.

From the moment we stepped through its doors, we were transported to a more gracious era. The traditional wooden architecture featuring intricate carvings, spacious courtyards with central pools, and airy verandahs created an atmosphere of timeless elegance. Each room has been thoughtfully renovated to provide contemporary comforts while preserving the original character of this heritage building.

The staff at Harivihar deserve special commendation for their attentive yet unobtrusive service. Their deep knowledge of the property's history and surrounding attractions enhanced our cultural understanding of the region. The warm, personalized attention made us feel less like guests and more like welcomed friends returning to a family home.

Culinary experiences at Harivihar were exceptional, with traditional Kerala cuisine served. The chefs excelled in preparing authentic local dishes, each meal was a delightful exploration of Malabar's rich culinary traditions.

The property's location provided a peaceful sanctuary while remaining accessible to Kozhikode's cultural attractions. After days spent exploring temples, sacred groves, and cultural performances, returning to Harivihar felt like coming home to an oasis of tranquility. The  birdsong at dawn, and the lush lawn created a multi-sensory retreat from the bustling world outside.

For those seeking optional wellness experiences, the in-house Ayurvedic treatments offered authentic therapeutic traditions in a serene setting.

What ultimately distinguishes Harivihar is its successful integration of heritage conservation with genuine hospitality. Unlike some heritage properties that feel like museums where you happen to sleep, Harivihar strikes the perfect balance—honoring its architectural legacy while ensuring guests experience the comfort, service, and amenities expected of a luxury retreat.

For travelers seeking to immerse themselves in Kerala's cultural richness while enjoying exceptional comfort and service, Harivihar Heritage Retreat in Kozhikode stands as a superlative choice—a destination in itself rather than merely a place to stay.
reviews.author: pradeep chakravarthy

reviews.text: "I had the most delightful stay at Harivihar! From the moment I arrived, the hospitality was warm and welcoming. The staff went above and beyond to ensure my comfort, making me feel right at home. The food was absolutely delicious – every meal was a treat! It truly felt like a peaceful retreat, and the atmosphere was perfect for relaxing. I highly recommend Harivihar for anyone looking to enjoy a cozy, homely stay with top-notch service and food. Authentic Kerala home where one feels like they’re not away from home. Can’t wait to return!"
reviews.author: Aiswarrya Dave

reviews.text: A rare, authentic Kerala ‘tharavad’ beautifully maintained and preserved. Warm and personal attention from the team. This made me nostalgic for my own family tharavad home that was sold years ago. Close to the town and all the great food in Calicut.
reviews.author: Mohan Prabhakar

tranquil-resort-wayanad.editorial: Laid-back rooms & treehouses on a tranquil coffee/spice plantation offering free breakfast & dinner.

reviews.text:
An exceptional and truly memorable stay. The amazing treehouse accommodation was beautifully designed and offered a sense of privacy, peace, and tranquillity. The setting provided an ideal environment to fully unwind, with really enjoyable walks through the coffee plantation and stunning views of the surrounding nature. The food was outstanding throughout, and the hospitality was warm, and highly personalised.

A personal thank you to Ajay, Nisha, and all of the amazing staff for making the experience so special. A standout stay that I would highly recommend to anyone seeking a high-quality, tranquil retreat.
reviews.author: Paul Busa

reviews.text: We chose Tranquil to celebrate our 30th wedding anniversary & were not disappointed. Ajay & Nisha gave us the warmest welcome & we were spoilt by the lovely staff. The scrumptious buffet was walked off on the many trails around the estate. A true haven of peace & tranquility to enjoy all nature has to offer. We didn't want to leave this little piece of paradise. Thank you Ajay & Nisha for such a wonderful memorable holiday.
reviews.author: james tweed

reviews.text: We had a fantastic 3 days at Tranquil Resort. An exceptional and beautiful place and a real chance to escape and relax. There are so many well marked walking trails through the coffee plantations and above the house - stunning views everywhere. We had a great early morning walk  around the estate with a bird expert and saw so many colourful birds and also the giant Malabar squirrels. There's a lovely pool and a games room. Every meal was a delicious feast. Ajay and Nisha are wonderful hosts - so warm and friendly. All the staff are lovely and nothing is too much trouble.  Highly recommended!!
reviews.author: Lucinda Green

reviews.text: It is a very nice huge plantation spread across 400 acres. Has many numbers of walking trails to suit most campers !! Food is excellent for both Veg and nonveg!!!
reviews.author: Gopal Sunder rao

reviews.text:
I wanted to share my wonderful experience at Tranquil, a homestay on the Kapamudi Coffee Estate in Wayanad. As someone who frequently visits homestays across the Western Ghats due to my love for nature and mountains, Tranquil truly stands out, largely due to the exceptional hospitality of its hosts, Nisha and Ajay.

Their personal touch makes a significant difference. From a refreshing cold towel upon arrival to their genuine interest in every meal, their commitment to guest satisfaction is evident. The rooms are spacious and impeccably clean, with crisp white linen and perfectly ironed towels. The overall cleanliness, even in the expansive 400-acre property, was impressive—I encountered virtually no bugs or insects, which was a pleasant surprise.

The staff also deserves high praise; they are humble, kind, and attentive, a testament to Nisha's excellent training. The food was cooked to perfection, contributing greatly to the overall delightful experience.

I highly recommend Tranquil to anyone seeking a top-tier homestay experience.
reviews.author: Joel Fernandez

kairali-ayurvedic-healing-village.editorial: Serene wellness resort with 2 vegetarian restaurants, plus yoga & Ayurvedic treatments.

reviews.text:
Dear Team Kairali,

I would like to express my heartfelt gratitude for the days I spent at your Ayurvedic health and spiritual retreat in India. Although my stay was short, it was deeply meaningful and transformative.

During my detox, I learned to truly respect my body, understand its rhythm, and connect with the profound love and faith that are so present in Indian culture. I was touched by the millenary wisdom of Ayurvedic medicine and the genuine care with which every treatment was offered.

This experience brought me healing, clarity, and inner peace. I am truly grateful to each member of your team for their dedication, kindness, and spiritual guidance.

I would like to let you know that I will definitely return soon, this time for a longer period, so I can continue this journey of health, balance, and self-discovery in your beautiful sanctuary.

With all my appreciation and respect,
Taiuska Villa de Lima
reviews.author: Taiuska Villa de Lima

reviews.text:
Dear Kairali Team,

I recently took a 9 days detox program with Kairali. It was my 1st solo trip and 1st time to any Ayurvedic Center. It was my pleasure staying at village and it was very impressive.

The property very big, very well maintained, clean and lush green. It weather was very good, clean air and full if oxygen.  The vibes were healing and peaceful.

I am very grateful to front desk team, guest relationship manager (sheeji), My doctor and other doctors, treatment team, Chefs and canteen staffs and yoga teachers. All staff were very diligent, kind, caring and well spoken.

The day was full of activities and i definitely recommend this place to others. It has been very useful to me specially learning Yoga, meditations and ayurveda dincharya.

Thanks & regards
Jita Gandhi
reviews.author: Jita Gandhi

reviews.text: It was my 1st trip to Kairali Ayurvedic healing village as a solo traveller for a short trip to get an Ayurvedic experience. I have been highly impressed by everything from the high hygiene standards of this property to the friendliness and professionalism of all the staff. The Ayurvedic treatments were top notch from the knowledge and thoroughness of the treatment staff to the cleanliness of the treatment rooms. The days were structured with yoga and meditation classes and instructor was very knowledgeable. Food served was healthy, wholesome but still tasty. The hotel setting is in a lush green canopy of trees and plants interspersed with a flowing stream which created a serene and peaceful environment. You certainly leave here feeling peaceful. Would highly recommend Kairali for your next Ayurvedic experience.
reviews.author: Raj Sharma

reviews.text:
I was at Kairali for 2 weeks and my experience was mixed.

If you are coming here for pure Ayurveda, then this place is amazing. I would recommend you to only come with an expectation of pure Ayurveda. The doctors are really professional and treatments are appropriate.
The masseurs are great (barring 1-2). Would like to compliment Devdas here.

Would also like to compliment the yoga teacher Sivaraman. He is very learned and experienced.

Food: Ayurveda is all about maintaining a healthy satvic diet. So they do adhere to that. However what I felt was that the food got very monotonus after sometime. Like all curries tasted exactly the same. I would have expected some variation here. On most days, they only served gourds (ash, bottle, ridge, bitter, snake). However the variety of soups were good.

My main complaint here is about the accomodation and service. The room tariffs are not at all justified. At 22000₹ a night, the rooms had the following issues:

1. A person with a height of 6'1 cannot fit on the bed as it was too short.

2. The charging ports were so old that that they didn't work without an adapter. We are in 2026.

3. The epoxy flooring had dents and grooves which made the room look extremely cheap.

4. There was not study desk or a working table. People stay here for long durations, this is very important for working people.

5. Room service and housekeeping was a major problem. The housekeeping never cleared used utensils. This is for the first time I am seeing that housekeeping is not concerned about utensils. When I complained, they just kept the utensils outside my room which attracted ants. And upon complaining again, it was never rectified.

6. The furniture was very tough to use and seemed that it has worn out. Drawers were not opening properly.

7. They had an amphitheatre, but there was not a single cultural event or show there during 2 weeks. I think they should organise ice breakers and collaborative events here because all guests are staying for a long term. I wonder why it is there. It got boring after a point staying alone.

8. They have a very tranquil stream by every room but the water is still here attracting all kinds of insects. They only open the flow of the water for an hour in the morning. I would recommend them to have the flow 24x7 so it actually feels like a stream and does not get stagnant attracting all kinds of pests near the room.

I wouldn't have even mentioned these points if the price wasn't this steep. But at the price point Kairali is operating I expect no slack in the quality of accomodation.

Some suggestions for improvement:

1. Having a digital conceirge application that collates all the information about my stay, health, diet, weekly menu, requests, support in one place accessible from everywhere.

2. Immediate renovation of all rooms (or reducing the prices to justify the quality offered.

3. Rethinking about the menu to make it more diverse. I am sure there can be innovation in Satvik food as well.

4. Having a souvenir shop/museum in the premises where guests can purchase artefacts, collectibles and essentials.

5. There are many treks and national parks. For a solo guest, renting their car is very expensive. Kairali can instead conduct fixed group departures to some of these nature oriented experiences at reduced costs.
reviews.author: Amish Agarwal

reviews.text:
This was truly a blessed experience. We had an unexpected turn of events with me falling sick on the first day and the doctors at the center were available late night also to check my condition. The kitchen staff were amazing in their service in sending food to the room and later to the hospital.

I would absolutely recommend this place for the treatments, the soul food and the affectionate staff
reviews.author: koka narasimharao

malabar-house-fort-kochi.editorial: Individually decorated quarters in a genteel hotel featuring an airy restaurant & an outdoor pool.

reviews.text:
A very charming restored colonial building for our short stay, the decor and rooms are filled with local art pieces.

We stayed 2 nights and also had breakfast and dinner there. The room was charming - bed was comfortable, but a slightly awkward high bed frame to get in and out of, and bathroom was big, water pressure could have been a bit stronger though.

The hotel was really great in helping with a reasonably priced room decoration package to surprise my wife for her birthday and also sorted out a cake for us.

Food was very nice for breakfast and for dinner there in the courtyard restaurant.
reviews.author: Jason Chan

reviews.text: Where do I begin? It's a beautiful building in the perfect location, and it could have been an excellent choice for our 24-26 December stay. But...the wheels fell off pretty quickly! All of the rooms are windowless (it's how it is designed), and while beautiful, they feel cold and lack cosiness. I was there with my sister, so we had a twin room, the beds were comfortable, but narrow. We were booked in for mandatory Christmas meal (not cheap!), but were berated (by a very unfriendly male member of staff) for not stating that we had a food allergy beforehand - not that we were ever asked, and apparently it couldn't be adjusted on a case-by-case basis as everything was preprepared. The musicians that played traditional music for the duration of the dinner were too loud, adding further stress to the whole evening. There is so much else to say, but I just don't want to be mean. Let's just say, we won't be going back there to stay or eat.
reviews.author: Julie Baxter

reviews.text:
Had sadya here about a year and a half ago on Onam. While roaming around Fort Kochi, I happened to come across this place and decided to try it — and it turned out to be an amazing experience.

Everything was good, and both the payasams were especially exceptional. The staff was good, and the place was beautifully arranged in a traditional way for Onam, which made the experience even more special.
reviews.author: Manu Bhadauriya

reviews.text: Double the price- half the quality. After 3 weeks in Sri Lanka and Kerala this "fine dining" experienced was the most dull of all meals I had- including the one for the 10th of the price. We had six daft plates - all overcokked and underdelivered. The blue cheese polenta had a thick layer of some white non. cheesy pudding over it and the seabass was overcooked and half the size of what my fiends had. Our eyes started to roll when the neighbouring table got a huge pile of calamari- the same we ordered that came in small tiny boring pieces. You simply do not do like that! We had to ask for napkins and the luke warm wine needed 15 min to be delivered. On top of that there was some INdian admittedly good  musician playing but they played so loud we could not have a conversation .My 50 rupee panu puri on the beach the day before was a more pleasant experience.
reviews.author: Helen Ingvarsson

reviews.text: We went to have dinner at Malabar House, this evening. They told us that we would get a table in 30 minutes, at 8.30pm. We reconfirmed it with the staff after waiting for 5-10 minutes. We were waiting in the lobby, amidst mosquitoes. When I went to ask them at 8.25pm, I was informed that no table was available. This is extremely unprofessional and represents very poor quality of service.
reviews.author: Sonal Shah

reviews.text: One of the best Ayurvedic retreats I have been to.  Wonderful doctors and treatments, delicious food, and a peaceful setting.  All of the staff are so friendly and go out of their way to make sure that you are feeling relaxed and supported.  I will definitely be back.
reviews.author: Natasha Sikand

reviews.text:
Authentic and holistic ayurvedic treatments, yogic wellness and healing, nutritious and organic diet, friendly and warm hospitality in a serene, beautiful ambience. As a result, i have better health and feel happier.

My special thanks to Dr Firoz, Sunny, Pranav, Nishant, Vipin, Anirudh, Ashish and Manjusha. And a sincere appreciation to every member of the team. I wish you all good health and happiness.

With lots of love and gratitude,

Paresh G Sangani
reviews.author: Paresh Sangani

reviews.text:
don't go by the hospital name.

It's a very upscale limited access kalari ayurveda centre.

Run by CGH Earth, committed to sustainability and social causes, this one too is no different.

Being Ayurveda, every plant, lawn n trees out there has medical values. Nothing exists which doesn't go with the core theme.

With its own ayurvedic herbs garden and organic vegitable garden along with a Zero waste policy, kalari Rasayana Paravur is an example for 'everything is possible'.

this limited capacity ayurveda treatment center has its standards set way high, only the uber rich can afford the cost. That's the cost of Ayurveda at its purest form.
reviews.author: Arun Muralidhar

reviews.text: Pl.send me your wich mediclaim policy in cashless
reviews.author: Mohammedi Chhatariya

reviews.text: Good ayurveda treatment center and hospital
reviews.author: Ajay Ks

swaswara-gokarna.editorial: Relaxed villas in a tranquil retreat with sea views & an Ayurvedic spa, plus yoga & meditation.

reviews.text: Had the most amazing experience at Swaswara. For the first time, we did a wellness retreat and it was the best! Staff was very warm, friendly and professional. Food was great, there were many wellness activities to do and the resort itself was beyond beautiful. Beach was just 5 mins away, swimming pool was pristine, rooms were so comfortable. The staff ensured the stay was absolutely wonderful at every point. Would definitely visit again :)
reviews.author: Sakshi Dangayach

reviews.text: Absolutely incredible, I did the rejuvenation program with two daily treatments. I can only recommend! Rooms, treatments, food and the entire hotel was outstanding. My therapist Sudheer did an amazing job, so professionalism knowledgeable. The villas are entirely outdoors which is so unique and really calms your nervous system.
reviews.author: Leo M.

reviews.text:
CGH Earth SwaSwara is a lovely , peaceful and calm wellness resort .
They have a really very good Aryuvedic and Naturopathy centre run with a  very well qualified team of practitioners and staff .
The ethos behind it is to either have a leisure break with treatments or retreat breaks .
Based on 26 acres of land , boosting their own organic garden , from which daily fresh fruits and vegetables are picked and freshly cooked to serve the guests .
The culinary experience was an absolutely awesome , . Kudos to the chefs and kitchen team .

Infact kudos to all the staff from restaurant, house cleaning , wellness centre , management are very friendly , always smiling and willing to help .

If one is looking for total quiet chill out time , then this is the place to be . No TV’s in the rooms either .
Only 24 Hut based suites , so not too many people present at any one time and restricted to over 12 years of age.

The pool was very nice and a whole range of activities such as yoga , meditation, art & crafts , cooking classes , vegetable picking , amongst a few activities.

Aum beach is very near , and one can go for a nice walk through the property to the beach  .
It is a public beach , and early morning is recommended as it is less crowded.

The only positive feedback that I would suggest to the management is to update their property in terms of all decor including furnishings and well ness centre as it is a bit outdated.
reviews.author: Sushma Patel

reviews.text:
Me and my wife stayed here for 5 days, we chose this resort to celebrate our anniversary, and it turned out to be one of the most delightful experiences we’ve had.

From the moment we arrived, the staff made us feel genuinely welcome — warm, attentive, and always eager to help. The lady at checkin (sorry for forgetting her name) gave us a detailed lay of the land, sadly we were not able to do everything the resort had to offer like naturopathy and ayurvedic treatments, daily yoga sessions (hopefully next time 😊)

What touched us most was the care they took in curating a special anniversary dinner just for us. I would like to mention the efforts of Mr. Shibin over here. He went out of his way to help make this occasion extra special. Every detail — the setting, the flavors, and the presentation — reflected such thoughtfulness and heart. It truly made our celebration unforgettable. Due apologies for not remembering the names but all the servers were very nice and helpful.

The property itself is exceptional — spacious, beautifully maintained, and perfectly situated amidst serene surroundings. Whether it was guiding us with local recommendations or attending to small requests, the team went above and beyond to ensure our comfort.

Everything about our stay felt effortless and elegant, a perfect blend of hospitality, beauty, and peace. We left with full hearts and beautiful memories, already looking forward to returning.
reviews.author: Mohit Misri

reviews.text:
My husband and I had a fantastic week-long stay at SwaSwara. The villa was beautiful - we loved the open roof concept for maximum fresh air (bedroom and bathroom, except shower, are enclosed). Taking a shower in the open air with the purified rain water was a healing experience in itself. My hair and skin feel so nice after one week without chemicals and harsh water. All the amenities one needs are included, including a hair dryer.

The overall location and surroundings is ideal for wellness. My favorite place on the grounds was the Meditation Hill which overlooks Om Beach and is a serene oasis for yoga, meditation or just chilling and watching the birds and beach action. Lots of fun nature watching anywhere on the grounds (non-people bothering monkeys and lots of cute squirrels, frogs, birds and even a peacock).

The food is perfect for my palate - healthy, mild spice (you can specify mild, med, spicy for the general stay) and vegetarian plus fish. I generally only eat two meals a day at home but didn’t want to miss anything the chefs lovingly prepared with the veggies from the garden on the property, so I ended up eating quite a bit (for me) without gaining any weight and feeling great. The chefs are incredibly accommodating for any food allergies and preferences.

My husband and I had consultations with the Ayurvedic doctors at the beginning of the trip (even though we weren’t doing the specific Ayurvedic program) and received a custom massage treatment plan for the week. The massages were very relaxing and rejuvenating - thoroughly enjoyed them. The doctors were very helpful and caring.

I felt all of the staff went above and beyond to make sure we were happy and well taken care of. With only 24 villas, it was never busy or crowded, but there were also activities that brought people together if you want to meet people and make a few new friends. Would highly recommend joining a cooking class and making some pottery in the art studio, of course in addition to any yoga or meditation session.

Next time, I would like to try the Ayurvedic program or even the 21 day Panchakarma program which is more strict with the food and treatment options and also activities you can do (no swimming or exploring the town). I spoke with several people who had done these programs, and they said they felt completely rejuvenated (and their faces were literally glowing!) This is a gem of a place. Yes, the price tag is certainly higher than other Ayurvedic centers, but it felt more like a vacation than a hospital stay and that makes the extra cost worth it. We will definitely be back!
reviews.author: Lindsey Angisetty

reviews.text:
I visited Honey Valley a few years ago but I'm posting my review only now. The property is EXCELLENT. Mr Suresh is a wonderful host.

The rooms define simplicity and has all the basics one requires. Delicious food is served at fixed timings as a buffet and the rates are reasonable.

The property can be reached only a 4x4 and they have car parking at the entrance (bottom) of the property. There are 2 levels - level 1 is Chingaara which is the more premium offering and level 2 at a higher elevation is Honey Valley.

There's a brilliant private waterfall situated on the property too. Located close to Tadiyandamol peak too, a must-do trek whilst in Coorg.

Wake up early in the morning and you're greeted by mesmerizing greenery covered in mist which slowly clears out showcasing the unbeatable views right from your room window. Honey Valley layout is very well laid out with offerings for varied budgets and even solo backpacker rooms.

Such a beautiful place this! I often reminisce my time here - away from all the chaotic city life.

Can't wait to be back here again. My best regards to Mr Suresh and his wife :)
reviews.author: Eve Maria

reviews.text:
Perfect for a Digital detox... The stay was amazing with very cozy and clean rooms built in traditional style cottages, surrounded by lush green forest and coffee plantation this is a perfect spot to blend in with nature and get away from everything that is digital. The owner Mr. Suresh and his wife are amazing people. The staff very friendly. Not a single moment her felt like out of place.

Tourists beware! No 5G or even 4G network here. Calls on Airtel barely make it out, but I'd say that's a selling point.
reviews.author: Atul Bhat

reviews.text: We stayed three nights at Honey Valley and we loved it. The nature is so beautiful at this remote spot and the owners and the whole team were lovely and super helpful. We spent our time just looking and listening into the forest, walking around, reading and eating and it was very relaxing. And the food for breakfast, lunch and dinner was amazing and we will miss it very much back in Germany. We recommend Honey Valley to anyone who wants to simply enjoy nature and food.
reviews.author: A G

reviews.text:
Visited with my wife, sister, and brother-in-law, and we all had a wonderful time. This resort is perfect if you want to escape city life and be close to nature. Surrounded by lush greenery, you wake up to birds chirping, and it’s a paradise for birdwatching and herping. I spotted multiple frog species, two cat snakes, and even a Malabar Pit Viper!

Special thanks to the owner, Suresh Sir, whose knowledge of nature and warm hospitality, along with the friendly staff, made our stay truly memorable.  The private waterfall is another highlight.

Highly recommend this place for nature lovers seeking peace and serenity. Not meant for parties or nightlife—come here only if you genuinely love nature.
reviews.author: Arun Prakash

reviews.text:
We had a very unpleasant experience at this homestay due to the attitude of the staff. They were uncooperative and showed no consideration for guests’ basic needs. When we requested milk to be boiled for our infant, they flatly refused saying their kitchen is always busy preparing next meals. They also denied making watermelon juice for the baby, even though we clearly mentioned that it could be made from our own buffet share and later we provided the fruit for the juice. Such rigid and insensitive behavior is unacceptable in the hospitality business. The staff seriously lack customer service orientation and make the stay uncomfortable for families. Definitely not a baby-friendly place.

Families with infants definitely have to reconsider their decision before booking this place.

Food is also below average.

Although food is not included, they will bill for all the head counts irrespective of whether a person will have food in their restaurant or not.

Location of the stay is beyond words to describe. Mesmerising views and nature.

Price of the stay is affordable but with little higher budget we wouldn’t have faced these issues. We had booked this stay  in the last minute during Dussehra festival so we didn’t have much choices then. But I wouldn’t recommend this place to anyone.
reviews.author: Rajesh R

shreyas-retreat-bengaluru.editorial: Airy cottages in a serene resort offering yoga classes & a spa, plus free meals & drinks.

reviews.text:
Excellent experience worth a visit for ayurvedic treatments
Staff is beyond 5 star service
food is veg but amazing
Plan for at-least a week long stay
reviews.author: Aditya Santpur

reviews.text: Lovely food, ambience and. The Vegans in our group were also very happy. The choices were limited for them but food was delicious. You have choice of selection and way of cooking. Lovely and efficient service. We thoroughly enjoyed it
reviews.author: Bhuvan Gasyal

reviews.text:
I stayed at Shreyas over the recent Xmas holidays. Compared to my stay 4 years ago, my experience was mixed.
Pros:  The Ayurvedic oil massage was amazing.  The spa treatments are relaxing. I especially liked the steam room.  For the classes, I enjoyed the meditation instruction.

Cons:
My dining experience was subpar. There were few options for a light diet, as I had requested. I ended up just having toast and tea, and no protein options.  I missed the group dining set up from the prior stay, where I got to meet people from around the world. This time, the table set up felt isolating, and meals were quiet and the environment felt somewhat depressing. I learned that the retreat is focusing on treatment and discouraging socializing.

I enjoyed the cottage experience the last time, but there were upkeep issues this time. On my first day, Black ants were crawling on the toilet in my room. It required multiple follow-ups to get a change to a new room.

The yoga classes were okay. On my last day, I learned from another visitor, that there were more advanced yoga classes.

Overall, this retreat fits boutique, but no longer luxurious, in my experience.
reviews.author: Nina Agrawal MD

reviews.text:
Very pleasent and luxurious place.
We experience the excellent hospitality here.
Highly recommend for people of physical and mental illness & for busy professionals to take break here.
Yoga sessions here are amazing.
Very healthy and tasty food. Booked for 5 days but stayed for 10 days the place is magical
reviews.author: Rohit goyal

reviews.text:
Lovely stay with awesome service.
We can experience healthy organic food, yoga etc and a very good place to relax. You can select this without any doubt if you need to stay relax and calmly .... Loved it... :) :) :)
reviews.author: Arun Nair

old-kent-estate-chikmagalur.editorial: Refined forest lodge on a 19th-century coffee plantation, plus dining & trekking activities.

reviews.text:
We had a wonderful stay at Old Kent Estates & Spa. The property beautifully preserves its colonial history, and walking around the estate truly feels like stepping back in time. The heritage architecture and surroundings are very well maintained, which made the experience unique and memorable.

The food was excellent and the service was warm and attentive throughout our stay. The staff ensured we were comfortable and well taken care of. Overall, it was a peaceful and memorable experience, and we would definitely recommend it to anyone visiting Coorg.
reviews.author: Gajanan Kalburgi

reviews.text: Just spent a weekend at Old Kent Estate and it was absolute bliss 😊! The location is super serene and peaceful, perfect for a family getaway. The estate itself is well-maintained and has a lovely vibe. Housekeeping was top-notch, our room was spotless and the staff were super helpful. We enjoyed strolling around the gardens and relaxing in the evenings with some authentic coffee. Highly recommend it to anyone looking for a chill spot to unwind with family or friends.
reviews.author: Palaksha Shetty

reviews.text: We spent three night stay at Old Kent Estate and Spa and it was blissful. Our room was very wonderful abd has a beautiful view. We love the property it has nice positive vibe. Ee enjoed the coffee plantation trail.  The staff , manager all are wonderful,cooperative. If any one want to live in peace with nature then it's a home away from the home.
reviews.author: Rohan Borawke

reviews.text:
We recently visited Old Kent Estates for a family trip, and had a really memorable stay. It can sometimes be tricky to find a place that everyone in the family enjoys, but this place managed to keep all of us happy.

We really enjoyed the heritage feel of the property; it has a unique, old-world charm to it. Being a classic estate, it has its own subtle quirks, but our rooms were excellent, spacious, and very comfortable. The location is peaceful and serene, with beautiful views everywhere you look. While it doesn't have a ton of typical resort activities, we felt that’s exactly what makes it special. The relaxing atmosphere gave us the perfect chance to just slow down and unwind.

The staff was incredibly courteous and took good care of us, and we really enjoyed the food as well. It was a beautiful trip, and I’d recommend it to anyone looking for a quiet, slow-paced family escape. We'd love to come back again!
reviews.author: Amit

reviews.text:
We had a wonderful stay at Old Kent Estates & Spa. The property beautifully reflects its colonial history, truly making you feel like you’re stepping back in time.

The rooms were very well maintained, clean, and retained their old heritage charm. The entire property has a rustic vibe that adds to its unique character, and being so secluded, it offers a peaceful and relaxing escape.

One of the highlights of our stay was the morning plantation trail, where we got to understand the journey of coffee from bean to cup — a must-do experience for coffee lovers. We also spotted a variety of birds and even came across kopi luwak, which made the experience even more special.

Waking up to the sound of birds every morning was incredibly refreshing. The service throughout our stay was warm, attentive, and made us feel truly welcomed.

Also worth mentioning — we had one of the best espressos of our life here!

Overall, it was a peaceful and memorable experience, and we would definitely recommend it to anyone visiting Coorg.
reviews.author: Zaheer Bf

nrityagram-dance-village.editorial: Residential dance school offering training & performances in a relaxed, garden-filled setting.

reviews.text:
Serene place and a private dance school dedicated to reviving, nurturing odissi form of classical dance. They offer day scholar, residential and weekend dance courses.
200 Rs for visit on Fri, Sat, Sun - and a must visit to some who likes nature, nature architecture and a serene place in bengaluru
reviews.author: Sreeram Pinnamaraju

reviews.text:
Visited Nrithyagramam recently and really liked the overall concept and peaceful vibe of the place. It has a lot of potential to become one of the best cultural and leisure spots in Bengaluru. The atmosphere is calm, and the space feels unique compared to the usual city hangout places.

However, I felt the place is not maintained as well as it could be. There is definitely a lot of scope for improvement in terms of cleanliness, upkeep, and overall ambience. With better maintenance and attention to detail, this place can offer a much more premium and enjoyable experience for visitors. Hoping to see it improve further because the foundation and idea behind the place are genuinely good.
reviews.author: Sneha Suresh

reviews.text: It’s primarily intended for artists who are passionate about dance and folk traditions. We had a delightful experience, feeling peaceful and relaxed. This place is dedicated to the practice and nurturing of Odissi dance and is home to a school inspired by the Gurukula system located in a serene, green setting, it’s an ideal spot to spend quality time with friends and family.
reviews.author: kiran kumar

reviews.text: This location is about 30.km from Bangalore..10 axres of land is dedicated and staff house are available with dance halls like gurukul system.. children come daily from Bangalore also. Lush greenery cover with plenty of trees gives peaceful atmosphere.
reviews.author: Ragu raman

reviews.text: I think it was the most authentic, planned and beautiful places I have seen around Bangalore. The location itself is awesome. Also structure built for the dance training and residential quaters were very amazing. All natural and well maintained it's a gurukul in all it's senses. Do make sure you call the school before visiting. They have dance classes timing as well which allow visitors to view the students perform. There is a very nominal entry fee (₹50) which also covers information about the place.
reviews.author: A Sunil

siolim-house-goa.editorial: Polished hotel in a 1675 Portuguese manor with elegant rooms & suites, plus fine dining.

reviews.text: Shivali and her lovely polite and ever vigilant team, made us feel so welcomed and relaxed in this shabby chic style boutique 10 roomed hotel, with a nice breakfast and snack and salad menu. Perfect for a short restful break on your tour of Goa, the swimming pool is a real highlight as it is quite large for such a small venue. Laid back, but very close to excellent array of high end restaurants, fabulous beaches and about 90 minutes drive to the airport. Overall an overly place to stay!
reviews.author: Mary Keane-Dawson

reviews.text: The building is over 300 years old. The rooms are large and have antique furniture and the entire vibe is around the age of the building, with the quite spaces adding to the tranquility. The have a beautiful courtyard with a well, which is an experience in itself. They also have a small pool which is good for a soak but not large enough for a workout. They have some seating around the pool and a gandola as well. There is a small massage room with a single bed. It's a must stay if you're a history lover, else the location can be better
reviews.author: Hannan Khan

reviews.text:
What a wonderful place to stay at! Beautiful architecture - a gorgeous property, super rustic and maintained amazingly.

The rooms are really warmly done up, full of character and yet luxurious and not imposing.
The breakfast is delicious, they have homemade bread, jam and other bakes, fresh juices and a special mention about their tea time - a variety of teas and special homemade bakes which were  honestly to die for! Soft buttery tea cakes and cookies. Yum yum yum.

It’s super quaint, with a lovely pool, and lots of places to lounge. Definitely recommend staying here.
reviews.author: Johann Vikram Singh

reviews.text: We recently spent 6 nights here for some relaxation after travelling elsewhere in India. The property is the epitome of the phrase “Heritage boutique hotel”. The team are really friendly and helpful with any requests. The food is excellent.
reviews.author: Steve Hoare

reviews.text:
This was our 3rd visit. Siolim House is located in the bustling town of Siolim - some 15/20 minutes by car from the nearest beach and although the village has developed over recent years it still retains a local feel. Siolim House is a beautifully restored property with an old world feel. It’s quirky, quaint and full of character. The upstairs bedrooms in particular are lovely; high ceilings, big bathrooms, four poster beds with mosquito nets, ceiling fans and a/c. The staff are welcoming and very helpful, many have been there a long time.  It’s within walking distance of a very good restaurant and a short taxi ride to a wider choice .
We really enjoyed our stay there and will without doubt return.
reviews.author: Stephanie

bhakti-kutir-palolem.editorial: Rustic huts in a serene hotel near the beach, plus an Ayurvedic treatment centre & open-air dining.

reviews.text: I just loved the vibe in this property, you ill feel like staying in jungle. Staff is very much co-operative and responsive. The only problem is, you might face the bugs, flies and crickets in the room, we had to change our room as in the night the room was full of bugs and which is quite understandable looking out the location, however on our request they have change our room and then we did not face this issue, overall the place is worth to stay, highly recommended.
reviews.author: Sanket Jadhav

reviews.text:
Pictures look fancier than what it actually is, that's why I am a bit disappointed. The rooms are not secluded or private, most of them are very visible or right next to each other. Open walls so bright and noisy a at 6.30 am. I stayed in a dorm and the mattress was thin and hard, bunk bed was so weak that it was shaking all night with normal body movements, very dirty from check in, a daily bathroom cleaning would be appreciated but it didn't happen as the same dead bugs and previous guest underwear were there before I arrived and after I left 3 days later. No lock on the door. No light or toilet paper in the toilet. They assigned me the top bed even though there was no one on the bottom bed. Not for light sleepers.
Locations is good, walkable to two beaches and surrounded by nature. I think they put a lot of attention to the private rooms and the experience in the dorms is not good enough. Wifi didn't work every morning.
It feels like camping but you payed for a room.
I arrived during check in times and they made me wait because the guy from reception was having dinner.
Good things: there is a Shala you can use on your own or join the classes everyday.
reviews.author: Valeria

reviews.text: I have paid 2200 INR for a dorm, I was expecting it to be a premium dorm. But its the most shadiest place I have ever stayed in. The reception is totally in a separate building very inside than the dorms.  dorms are in the first floor in a different building and the wooden stairs are so steep and narrow to safely climb. The beds are literally the metal beds from Government hospitals.. The washrooms are on the ground floor. Kitchen feels like second hard garage with all used broken items.. Very disappointed with this stay. I dont feel safe to stay here, hoping for the night to end soon. The pictures on the internet are no where close to how they actually look.
reviews.author: tejaswini manohar

reviews.text: Such a lovely place! You are staying in a lush green environment with beautiful jungle sounds. There’s yoga class every morning, opportunity to book an Ayurveda massage or treatments and a cozy café where they serve delicious and fresh cooked foods, coffee, tea and chai. The staff is also very kind and helpful. Minor issue was the intense heavy rains for big parts of the day and night that caused some dripping in our room, but the team was on it to fix it immediately. The location is very close to the beach, town and has plenty other cafés and stores in its surroundings.
reviews.author: Eva Dusch

reviews.text:
The stay is fine if you book a cottage yet cannot make sure of good experience. As they don't care much about Indian visitors and focus more on foreigners. I had booked both- cottage in one visit and dorm in another. My visit in the cottage was for a day, which I liked being surrounded by trees. So, I booked the dorm in next visit for 6 days. I only stayed for 5 though and checked out early due to my experience.

What added to my horrid experience was-
1. Dorms are not worth even half their price: Frogs float in the toilets and you can't even pee. 5 days in a row, I saw a frog sticking to the toilet rim. A slug crawling up in the sink. That's clearly not how you 'let the nature' surround you, it's sheer carelessness in the disguise of 'living with nature.' I have stayed in super cool tent houses near the core region of national parks, never faced such issues. I'm aware when it's called nature surrounding you and what's just marketing.
2. Geysers don't work more than 5 seconds of warm water.
2. Dorm beds are too feeble to climb up that they keep shaking throughout the night. If you take the upper bed, you get to hear the toilet sounds nobody wants to hear.
3. Surrounding noise: The entire surrounding keeps buzzing with noise till late night and then early morning.
3. Inclusivity and customer experience : The place lacks consistent customer satisfaction. Like I sensed that the cafeteria prefers to serve foreigners first and expect you to accommodate by giving the leftovers like I ordered idli sambhar and was given the left over dal as sambhar was not left post their buffet. Next day dosa was overly cooked. Everyday I used to order first and was served last. Once I requested to reheat my tea, they mentioned that they don't have an option for that. Felt weird. One of my dorm mates was an annoying old English lady who used to comment on every visitor like we still owe her Lagaan. (This might come across as a joke but she really made sure nobody sticks there for long, she would keep creating rules restraining others to move freely like not switching on bathroom lights post evening. It was super annoying.)
The place lacks inclusivity and seems like they prefer to host non-indians more.
reviews.author: Iti Shukla

reviews.text:
My girlfriend and I were at the YogaMagic during the monsoon season. That was really special because we were the only guests for most of the week and quickly felt like part of a family. Outside it was totally green and meditative. We were yoga beginners and meditation didn't play a big role in our lives. The classes were made for us and the teacher were very gentle and what can I say? It was simply great, so that we are now totally enthusiastic "yogis" and incorporate yoga into our everyday lives. The food was really incredible delicious and the staff was very helpful and kind. The plant was just spraying with energy, which was certainly also due to the rainy season, as everything was green and full of life. I really want to recommend this retreat. It was one of the greatest experiences during my travels in the last 10 years. We definitely want to come back during the dry season.

overall impression
perfection
reviews.author: Dennis Hehr

reviews.text:
Yoga magic is a nice, relaxing, clean yoga retreat in Goa. The room we had is amazing. It is made of natural materials and very very clean. The staff takes very good care of the rooms and use smoke and everything to keep mosqitos outside.
I was relly impressed by the fact that Yoga magic almost produce no plastic and heavy trash and mostly use natural and as far we recognized organic products. This is a rolemodel place for India, because India has a huge trash problem. In my opinion this is a big support for Indian natives to change  something in the country.
The food was amazing. I didnt havy any stomach issues during my stay. The diahes were very tasty. The kitchen was amazing clean.

Some people mentioned in other reviews that the prices are too high. I strongly disagree, because one have to keep in mind that the value of a sustainable  retreat with fair paid staff are not easy to provide in India and I can only imagine how difficult this is in India. A 5 star resort might give you more !luxury! comfort, but I guess it also produces plastic trash and have 100 low paid staff members (thats what we recognized in most higher price accomendations).
reviews.author: Max S

reviews.text: A beautiful and peaceful retreat, created with a passion for nature and spiritual peace. Ishi and Phil are wonderful hosts and are really welcoming. The food is fabulous and my only regret is I didn't stay longer. Can't wait to return.
reviews.author: Stephen Saleh

reviews.text: Got a taxi here for the 1630 drop in class advertised on the website to be told on arrival, that they do not offer drop in classes. I even tried all numbers which did not work. Waste of time and money
reviews.author: Liquid Steak

reviews.text:
Amazing food
Wonderful accommodation
Excellent yoga tuition (and patience!)
Location
Outdoor pool and area to chill out
Staff 😊
reviews.author: danielle brookes

house-of-mg-ahmedabad.editorial: Refined heritage hotel featuring a fine dining vegetarian restaurant & a lotus-themed indoor pool.

reviews.text: Had a wonderful dining experience at Agashiye. The rooftop heritage ambiance, traditional Gujarati hospitality, and authentic unlimited thali make this place truly unforgettable. Every dish was fresh, flavorful, and beautifully served with attention to detail. The staff was warm and courteous, adding to the overall royal experience. Perfect place to enjoy authentic Gujarati cuisine in the heart of Ahmedabad. Highly recommended for food lovers and anyone wanting a cultural dining experience!  🎉🥳🤩☺️🏡
reviews.author: Khushbu

reviews.text:
After staying in hundreds of hotels in different towns, cities and on different continents and I can solidly say this is one of the best places I have ever stayed in my life.

The ambience, the service, the quality of the food, the care taken to maintain and upkeep the property, the sheer beauty and elegance of the hotel in its entirety.

An incredible incredible experience. I would come back to this city to stay solely here and experience the food, vibe and atmosphere all over again
reviews.author: Leona Whitfield

reviews.text: I had a wonderful experience with the House of MG.  I was part of a team hosting a two-week long international conference in Ahmedabad in early December and we selected this hotel for our faculty and many participants.  Excellent choice.  The hotel has an unexpected charm, very different from the typical high-end chain hotel.  An old-world experience with modern conveniences and an amazing staff who made us feel at home.  Many thanks to the patient Mr. Praveen who I worked with over several months to make all of the bookings.  Thank you to Raza and others at the front desk for always welcoming me each day.  And a special thanks to Andria at the front desk for her kindness, good company, and willingness to jump in to a variety of situations to make sure our guests were well taken care of at the hotel. I also enjoyed meeting Abhay, the hotel owner, on many occasions, at the hotel and around Ahmedabad.  He was committed to making sure our conference guests had a great experience at the hotel and in Ahmedabad.
reviews.author: Brian Feagans

reviews.text:
The Green House - The House of MG, and it continues to be one of the best places to enjoy authentic food in the city.

The menu truly stands out—almost every dish is thoughtfully prepared and full of flavor. While it is a bit on the expensive side, it’s completely justified given the heritage ambiance, quality of food, and the overall experience. Being part of The House of MG, it also attracts tourists, which adds to its charm.

The indoor pool setting is absolutely mind-blowing and creates a very unique dining atmosphere. Since it’s close to my office, I visit regularly, and it never disappoints.

Today, I tried the cold coffee with vanilla ice cream—total satisfaction! A perfect blend and very refreshing.

If you’re looking for authentic Gujarati food and snacks, this place is a must-visit. Even their packed lunch menu is excellent in taste and quality.

They also serve infused water with cucumber and lemon, which is a great touch. It’s not just refreshing but also has health benefits—helps in digestion, keeps you hydrated, and gives a light detox feel. A small detail, but it really enhances the overall dining experience.

Overall: A heritage dining experience worth every visit.
reviews.author: Meet Raval

reviews.text:
The House of MG is less a conventional hotel and more a lived-in heritage residence, where history feels present rather than preserved behind glass. Originally built in 1924 as a private mansion, the property has been thoughtfully restored into a boutique hotel that retains its architectural character while integrating modern comforts with restraint.From a luxury traveller’s perspective, the sense of arrival is immediate and distinct. The energy of the old city gives way to a calm, inward-looking courtyard framed by carved wood, vintage textiles, and period detailing. The hotel remains intimate in scale, with individually styled rooms that are spacious, high-ceilinged, and reflective of its residential origins rather than standardised luxury formats.
Service is warm and personal, leaning away from rigid formality. Interactions feel genuine, often extending into thoughtful recommendations and subtle gestures that enhance the overall stay. There is a quiet attentiveness that aligns well with the property’s character.
Dining is integral to the experience, particularly the rooftop restaurant Agashiye, where traditional Gujarati cuisine is presented with care and authenticity. Across the property, food feels rooted and contextual rather than designed for spectacle.
Amenities such as a small indoor pool, wellness offerings, and curated textile elements add depth without distracting from the heritage narrative.
This is not luxury defined by scale or extravagance. Instead, it is immersive, textural, and culturally grounded—best suited for travellers who value character, intimacy, and a strong sense of place.
reviews.author: Rajveer Singh Prataap

reviews.text:
Simply superb. Spent lots of time with family and took many photos.
Very nicely landscaped garden and many selfie points.
reviews.author: Milap Patel

reviews.text: Handloom is good, but price is so high. Nothing local benifits. If you want to see craft making process then will be good.
reviews.author: Sangeeta Keshari

reviews.text: Great Original Handicraft where you can see live hathvanat by their experts. Lessor shows & nice museum. Must to see while on Bhuj Tour. Thanks to Ashapura Foundation.
reviews.author: Devang R Bhatt

reviews.text: Best village for local handicraft items.best quality
reviews.author: Yogeshkumar Parmar

reviews.text: Lots of clothes and handicraft to choose from. The eye to detail is amazing and the craftsmanship is incredible. Shop owners almost all of them are extremely polite humble and welcoming. You can bargain the price if buying in bulk. The market is  losed for lunch so be aware. Monday can also be closed as a holiday however few shops are open.
reviews.author: K R

reviews.text:
The tent city is a much hyped place for last two decades or so. With the help of Amitabh Bachhan's recommendation and very far sighted visionary, dear PM, Narendra Modi's efforst, the place gets visited by thousands of tourists from all over the world and from various states of India as well.

The infrastructure, administration and management are par excellence. Every minute requirement of tourist is anticipated and attended to.

However, the company seems to be believing in giving priority to their profit at the cost of comfort and convenience of the customer.

The tent rooms are dirty, linens old and torn off and the wash room area is below standard of a non-star hotel.

The company knows that the tourists are going to pour in thousands even if they offer third class services at a 5 star+ cost and hence, managing to loot huge profits.

I am convinced that if the services are hired from well known companies like Taj, Oberoi or ITC, they would offer much better experience to a visitor with a nominal increase in charges or even at the same charges.
reviews.author: Pankaj Shukla

reviews.text: Very nice tents with all facilities. Had good experience as we went on full moon day. Tasty dinner and nice campfire environment at night.
reviews.author: Supriya Sankpal

reviews.text: There was no non-vegetarian food. The vegetarian food was also bland. 🤬
reviews.author: Rahul Narwal

reviews.text: My experience is good and tent sity is also good
reviews.author: Yash Goswami

reviews.text:
I not spend there
This is only my personal opinion 😎😎
reviews.author: Mukul Nainwal

reviews.text:
We stayed there for two nights 22nd and 23rd May 2026 in the STILTED COTTAGE (no.4) in front of the beautiful Ken river.
