reviews.author: Rahul Tripathi

reviews.text:
We spent 7 unforgettable nights at Forsyth Lodge, and it was nothing short of exceptional. During our stay, we went on 14 safaris, each one thrilling and beautifully organized. The wildlife experiences were outstanding, and the guides were knowledgeable, patient, and deeply respectful of the forest.

The lodge itself is stunning - peaceful, luxurious, and perfectly blended into its natural surroundings. Every detail felt thoughtfully curated, from the comfort of the rooms to the serene atmosphere throughout the property.

The food deserves special mention. Every meal was delicious, varied, and beautifully presented. After long safaris, coming back to such incredible cuisine was a real highlight of our days.

Above all, what truly elevated our experience was the hospitality. Nipun, our host and GM, was exceptional- warm, attentive, and genuinely passionate about ensuring we had the best possible stay. From safari coordination to personal touches that made us feel completely at home, Nipun went above and beyond in every way.

Forsyth Lodge is a rare gem that combines world-class wildlife experiences with outstanding comfort and heartfelt hospitality. We left with incredible memories and cannot recommend it highly enough. We would return in a heartbeat.
reviews.author: Gautam Cn

reviews.text: A place I want to go back to again and again. Satpura is nature in its most abundant, magnificent form and Forsyth makes sure you don't miss that. The rooms are very comfortable and spacious. The food was an absolute delight -  delicious, fresh with new flavors everyday.  Every meal had a unique chutney we had never had before. They even served warm-from-the-oven cookies! Kudos to the team and I hope they continue to keep up the very high standards
reviews.author: Saumya Saraogi

reviews.text:
Our recent family vacation at Forsyth Lodge was nothing short of extraordinary. From the moment we made the booking until the day we checked out, every detail was handled with warmth, professionalism, and genuine care.

The hospitality here is truly on another level. Every single time we entered or exited the property, we were welcomed with warm smiling faces, refreshing cold towels, and chilled lemon juice — such a simple gesture, yet it made us feel incredibly valued throughout our stay.

What impressed us even before arrival was their attention to detail. The team followed up regularly before our trip, checked on food allergies in advance, confirmed our travel progress, and even ensured we had no difficulty locating the property. That level of personalised service is rare to find.

The lodge itself is beautifully designed in a colonial style, blending luxury seamlessly with nature. The heart of the property, “The Den,” which houses the reception, library, and dining area, is thoughtfully crafted and creates such a warm and elegant atmosphere. Our rooms (Room 9 and Room 11) were absolutely mesmerising — spacious, beautifully maintained, and complete with lavish sit-out areas overlooking the forest. Sitting there quietly and absorbing the sounds of nature was an experience in itself. We were even lucky enough to spot peacocks while cycling through the property, which made the experience feel even more magical and connected to nature.

The spa experience also deserves a special mention. It was incredibly relaxing, professionally managed, and honestly an experience in itself — made even better by how modestly priced it was compared to the quality of service offered.

Spread across nearly 40 acres of forest land, with only a very small portion developed, the property maintains an untouched and immersive jungle experience. With only 14 rooms across the Machan and Gondia cottages, the exclusivity and peace are truly special.

Yes, the property is premium, but every penny spent feels completely justified. The food was exceptional — curated menus, beautifully cooked dishes, and service delivered with genuine affection and care by the entire staff. They also went out of their way to accommodate our special requests, which meant a lot to our family.

A special mention to naturalist Pankaj Pawan, whose knowledge and experience made our safaris unforgettable. We were fortunate enough to have tiger sightings in both the core and buffer zones, and one of the sightings — witnessing a tiger carrying away its kill — was an unforgettable moment for any wildlife enthusiast.

Every member of our family thoroughly enjoyed their stay, and honestly, we only wished we had planned for a few more days there. Forsyth Lodge is not just a place to stay — it’s an experience filled with warmth, nature, luxury, and memories that will stay with us forever.

Highly recommended for families, wildlife lovers, and anyone looking to disconnect from the chaos and reconnect with nature in the most beautiful way possible.
reviews.author: J J

reviews.text:
Forsyth Lodge in Satpura is a nature‑focused retreat that blends rustic charm with solid comfort. Its to  rave about the spacious, well‑appointed cottages (often with private sit‑outs and terraces) and the attentive staff Nipun, Faiz, Sanal, and others make you feel at home and go the extra mile on safaris and meals . Safaris, boat rides, and bird‑watching are top‑notch, thanks to knowledgeable naturalists who help spot tigers, leopards, bears, and countless birds

On the flip side, a few reviewers note that the price can feel high for a “rustic‑luxury” experience, and amenities like Wi‑Fi are limited to the dining area; mobile signal can be spotty (Vodafone none, Jio works, Airtel shaky) . Some guests mention occasional service lapses (e.g., limited snack options on safaris) and a small, less‑maintained pool
reviews.author: Seema Khaleel

reviews.text:
A Luxurious Escape in the Heart of Nature

Set near the beautiful Satpura Tiger Reserve, Forsyth Lodge offers an exceptional blend of refined luxury and sustainability.

This is an absolute luxurious abode while remaining deeply committed to preserving nature. Spread across acres of natural habitat, the property provides a rare sense of space and tranquility. The cottages are thoughtfully placed, ensuring privacy and a true immersion in the wilderness.

The rooms are spacious and beautifully designed, with inviting sit outs that allow you to fully enjoy the surroundings. The bathrooms are stunning and so thoughtfully done that they elevate even the simplest moments into a sensory experience.

The main lodge, the Den, is exquisitely curated with carefully chosen crafts and artwork, along with a charming library that carries a subtle colonial-era character, making it a perfect place to unwind between safaris.

The staff truly elevate the experience. Warm, attentive, and highly professional, they consistently deliver on their promise to make the stay seamless and comfortable. Service throughout reflects a genuine five-star standard, with an attention to detail that is both refined and effortless.

Dining is another highlight. The menu is thoughtfully curated each day, offering fresh, flavourful dishes crafted from locally sourced produce. The table service is impeccable, attentive without being intrusive, and always delivered with warmth and grace.

The safaris were impeccably arranged. A special mention to Mr. Pankaj, whose deep knowledge of wildlife and keen eye for detail transformed each outing into a rich and memorable experience.

The spa experience deserves equal praise, with top-notch treatments including a perfectly delivered deep tissue and Swedish massage that added a restorative dimension to the stay.

A heartfelt appreciation to Mr. Pankaj, our exceptional naturalist, Mr. Jitendra and Mr. Ravi for their gracious and attentive table service, Chef Mr. Krishna Raj Chaudhary for the consistently delicious and thoughtfully prepared cuisine, Mr. Vishram for the meticulous and caring room service, and Ms. Divya Berde for the smooth coordination, communication, and thoughtful follow-up throughout the booking process. Their individual contributions collectively elevated the experience to a truly outstanding level.

This is a place that offers a rare opportunity to fully immerse oneself in nature while enjoying comfort, elegance, and hospitality at its finest.
reviews.author: Jijo John

reviews.text: Amazingly peaceful and beautiful. One of the serene places to visit in MP. I would love to visit again and again Raj Rajeshwari Temple, a boat ride on Maa Narmada, offering “diya🪔”, sitting on the stairs at the bank of Maa Narmada River. Do visit temples in Maheshwar along with historical Ahilya Devi Fort, watching light show is great. Paid parking is also available. Public restrooms are near parking.
reviews.author: Chanchal

reviews.text:
Ahilya Devi Maheshwar Fort is a grand riverside fortress located on the banks of the sacred Narmada River in Maheshwar, Madhya Pradesh. This historic fort was once the royal residence of Queen Ahilyabai Holkar, the legendary ruler known for her justice, wisdom and devotion.

The fort is admired for its majestic stone architecture, peaceful surroundings, and breathtaking river views. Inside the fort complex are ancient temples, palace areas, courtyards, and a museum that displays the history and life of Ahilyabai Holkar. The ghats below the fort are extremely scenic, especially during sunrise and sunset.

Calm, spiritual, and full of historical charm, Maheshwar Fort is one of the most beautiful heritage destinations in Central India and a must-visit place for travelers, devotees, and history lovers.
reviews.author: mahendra sahani

reviews.text: Very beautiful place and have a historical background at the bank of Narmada river.within a half day tour any one can take part The Narmada Aroti in the evening and a very informative lesser show of the historic Maheshwar. Every body can enjoy boat ride at Narmada river especially at the sun set time. The renowned Maheswari silk Sarees are producing here and any one can buy it.
reviews.author: Debabrata Banerjee

reviews.text: Ahilya Devi Maheshwar Fort is a beautiful and peaceful place filled with history and divine energy. The architecture is stunning, and the view of the Narmada River adds to its charm. We also did darshan of Jagannathji and Parshuramji Mandir on the riverbank — both temples have a serene and spiritual atmosphere. The only issue we faced was finding proper parking for vehicles, as it gets a bit crowded. Overall, a wonderful and memorable experience! 🙏✨
reviews.author: Deepak Solanki

reviews.text:
Maheshwar Fort, located in Khargone district of Madhya Pradesh, is one of the most beautiful and peaceful historical places in central India. Situated right on the banks of the holy Narmada River, this fort is famous for its spiritual vibe, rich history, and stunning architecture.
The fort was developed in the 18th century by Rani Ahilyabai Holkar, one of India’s most respected and visionary queens. Maheshwar was once the capital of the Holkar dynasty, and this fort served as both a royal palace and an administrative center. Even today, parts of the fort are used by the Holkar royal family, which adds to its authenticity.
What makes Maheshwar Fort truly special is its perfect blend of history and spirituality. Inside the complex, you will find the Ahilyeshwar Mahadev Temple, dedicated to Lord Shiva. The stone-carved walls, narrow corridors, and old-style balconies reflect classic Maratha architecture. Below the fort lie the beautiful Narmada ghats, where pilgrims perform rituals and evening aarti creates a calm, divine atmosphere.
Maheshwar is also world-famous for Maheshwari handloom sarees, known for their elegance and durability. Visiting local weavers near the fort is a unique cultural experience.
The best time to visit Maheshwar Fort is from October to March, especially during sunrise or sunset, when the river and fort glow with golden hues. Overall, Maheshwar Fort is a must-visit destination for history lovers, spiritual seekers, photographers, and anyone looking for peace and heritage in one place. 🌅🙏

reviews.text: If you don't like Indian food, this is the place for you. Everyone else should probably avoid this restaurant. The setting is spectacular, the service attentive, but everything feels extremely Westernized. We've never eaten so badly and so blandly for so much money in India. But I suppose that's exactly what European tour groups like. What we could see of the hotel looks promising.
reviews.author: Woods Stock

reviews.text: We stayed here for one night so we could have dinner on the rooftop while our daughter slept downstairs. The view and ambiance is great, and the food is the best! Mohammed is a very energetic and dedicated local (Bundi) chef who served us great Indian dishes with nice additional twists that made them memorable. The hotel is also great and characteristic, and worth a stay, although not for the budget traveler.
reviews.author: Sjors van der Meer

reviews.text: Bundi Vilas Rooftop restaurant: We had dinner here on Christmas Eve. It was winter time with chilly evenings but the view of the fort made it worthwhile. It was a set menu, which was fine for us (it means less waste in the kitchen) Muhammed did a very good job in offering a balanced menu, we loved every dish of it. It tasted fresh, refined, Indian but not too spicy :) Probably the best view in town of the fort from the rooftop terras. You would expect with its prime location, that the view is the best thing here and the food is secondary.. well that's not true, the food is really good. Don't hesitate, this is your dinner place in Bundi!
reviews.author: Eva van Straelen

reviews.text: For anybody travelling to Bundi, looking for a great place to stay - look no further than Bundi Vilas, an absolutely beautiful haveli set right under the castle (opp motimahal). Peaceful atmosphere, beautiful views over the city, a wonderful bedroom and excellent service. A helpful  manager called Vishnu & a brilliant chef, Mohammad. The food was very fresh & beautifully done. Vegetarian but full of flavour. Dinner on the roof terrace both evenings with rose petals & close to the markets.
reviews.author: Sophie Ransom

reviews.text: We stayed here on a Wild Frontiers trip. Great location with fantastic views from the rooftop terrace. Food all veg and could be spicier, but very good. We had a cooking demo and lesson on the second night. Rooms characterful. Ours was 104 which I think was one of the best. Service was excellent and special mention for chef Mohammed.
reviews.author: Richard Drury

apani-dhani-nawalgarh.editorial: Cozy units with thatched roofs in a charming ecolodge with gardens, meals & a yoga hall.

reviews.text: If you come to this area then stay in Nawalgargh at Apani Dhani.  We have just had 4 nights there and found it to be a little piece of heaven. It is tranquil, with a beautiful courtyard tonsit and relax in.  The food is all organic and is grown in their garden.  We took the opportunity to take their cooking class, which started with picking the ingredients from their garden and was very hands on.  We had a guide, Dinesh, on a couple of days which meant we got the most of our time when exploring some of the nearby havelis and cenotaphs.
reviews.author: Graham B

reviews.text:
A serene rustic place with a rural setting. The owner Mr. Jangid has developed it over several years in stages. His son Gajendra and his wife, Gayatri manage it to give you an incredible experience of hospitality. It is a 2.5 acre farm where they grow their grains, vegetables, oil seeds, and have kept few cows also.
Rooms are clean and bathrooms are very good. Linen quality is excellent.
There is a covered sitting area in the centre where one can sit and chat endlessly. Local artists come and teach the traditional Bandhani dying skills to those who are interested.
Food is served according to the timings and very well presented. Since I cannot eat chilies, they prepared my meal separately to suit my palate. It felt so nice.
We happened to meet a couple from Australia that made our stay even more memorable.
It is a strictly vegetarian and no – alcohol place.
reviews.author: Sanjiv Pandey

reviews.text: Great place to stay while visiting Shekhwati region. Very peaceful with a rustic feel but with all the amenities Westerners like us expect. The home cooked meals were amazing - everything fresh from their own garden and great combinations of flavours. The green tomatoes dish we got is a contender for my top 10 dishes of all time. Overall highly recommended.
reviews.author: Lee Miller

reviews.text:
The best experience of our Rajasthan trip! The buildings, rooms exquisite. The philosophy they have about treading softly on mother earth, growing their own vegetables, recycling, not wasting anything, educating the visitor about their culture, what not to do was so good. As for the food absolutely delicious.  They are so helpful and kind. We had the most delightful 4 nights there. Plenty of things to do and see, organised if that is what you want and totally hassle free. You couldn't be in a better place.
A big heartfelt thank you Gapu and Gaitry. Marleen and Anne.
reviews.author: Barrie Peters

reviews.text:
Ramesh is an amazing person who created a perfect place in Nawalghar. He has a deep sense of responsibility and ethic and developed tourism in Nawalghar with a high level of professionalism.
The place is quiet, beautiful and well managed. The rooms are amazing and everything is well maintained and clean. His son prepares amazing local foods and offers cooking classes (many activities are available through the ecolodge).
We also took a day tour with a guide he referred to (the entire price goes directly to the guide, the ecolodge takes no commission), and the guide had a very high knowledge of everything you want to know about India and Havelis. He spoke perfectly french as well.
In other words, we highly recommend!
Thank you to Ramesh and his family for their warm welcome.
reviews.author: Xavier Zuinen

rohet-garh-jodhpur.editorial: Regal lakefront hotel featuring a restaurant, a spa & an outdoor pool, plus breakfast.

reviews.text: This 400 year old fort now a hotel is a tale of two parts. The outside spaces are delightful, spacious, with green lawns and comfortable seating. Our room (2) however was somewhat tired. The bed was uncomfortable and the bathroom in need of some basic improvements. Breakfast buffet is a simple offering but is perfectly acceptable as is the supper buffet. All in all our stay was pleasant but could have been magical.
reviews.author: Stephen Duke

reviews.text: I loved everything about this hotel, it was the highlight of my trip to India: the location in a pretty and very safe village with the friendliest local people, the magnificent gardens, the historic architecture, the decor that respected the heritage of the building, the helpful and cheerful staff.  Dinner was delicious, breakfast was good, the bar well stocked and very reasonably priced.  In my three weeks in India, I experienced  no greater joy than when sitting amongst the beautifully tended lawn, trees and petunias in the hotel garden, enjoying the traditional music with a gin and tonic in the evening.  Also, every morning started with a visit to the top terrace overlooking the lake, for a dose of peace and soulfulness before departing to see the Jodhpur sites nearby.  Special mention should also go to Mr Sharvah Das who runs the magnificent hotel shop.  I made many purchases there, buying most of my gifts and souvenirs, as the quality was way better than anywhere else.  Mr Das was extremely patient and helpful with his suggestions and the prices very reasonable for the quality.  I wish I could have stayed longer than the two evenings, but I thank everyone for making my stay quite magical.
reviews.author: Psipsina Corelli

reviews.text:
I recently stayed at Rohetgarh for a much-needed getaway, and it exceeded every expectation. From the moment we arrived, the staff went above and beyond to ensure we were comfortable.

The property itself is breathtaking. Our room had a stunning view of the Lake and was spotlessly clean with an incredibly comfortable bed. We spent most of our time relaxing exploring the property, which was peaceful.

The food was another highlight—the breakfast buffet had an amazing variety, and dinner was delicious. If you are looking for a place to disconnect and be pampered, I cannot recommend this place enough. We are already planning our return!
reviews.author: Chirag Khatri

reviews.text:
Very nice heritage property. Friendly people and very good service. They even have live music and bonfire in winters.
Location is 1 hour from Jodhpur.
Food is nice tasting but limited on breakfast menu.
reviews.author: Vineet Kashyap

reviews.text: The facilities you get for the cost they charge is very less you can get better facilities with the same rate at other places. The menu is very limited even at the new year Gala dinner they kept a very limited menu every other things were chargeable. We had much expectation with the gala dinner but were utterly disappointed. Would not recommend the property.

reviews.text:

⭐ Raghurajpur Heritage Artist Village, Odisha
🎨 A village where every wall tells a story… and every home is an art gallery.
Raghurajpur Heritage Village is a world-famous artisan village known for preserving Odisha’s traditional art, culture, and handicrafts. From intricate Pattachitra paintings to palm leaf carvings and handmade crafts, this peaceful village feels like a living museum of Indian heritage.
Recognized as India’s first heritage crafts village, Raghurajpur is home to generations of skilled artists who continue centuries-old artistic traditions.
🕘 Timings: 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM
🎟️ Entry: FREE
📍 ~15 km from Puri
✈️ Nearest Airport: Bhubaneswar (~55 km)
🚆 Railway Station: Puri
🌄 Best Time:
Morning & evening for comfortable exploration
Winter season (Oct–Feb)
🔍 5 Interesting Facts About Raghurajpur
1️⃣ India’s first officially recognized heritage crafts village.
2️⃣ Famous worldwide for traditional Pattachitra art.
3️⃣ Many national award-winning artists belong to this village.
4️⃣ Every house here is decorated with traditional paintings and murals.
5️⃣ Birthplace of renowned Odissi dance maestro Guru Kelucharan Mohapatra.
✨ Don’t Miss:
Live Pattachitra painting demonstrations 🎨
Palm leaf engravings & handmade crafts
Interacting directly with local artists
🙏 Do’s:
Support local artisans by purchasing authentic artwork
Respect artists and their work
Explore the village slowly to enjoy its beauty
🚫 Don’ts:
Avoid bargaining excessively with local artists
Don’t touch artworks without permission
💬 My Experience:
Walking through Raghurajpur feels like entering a different world where art, culture, and tradition are alive in every corner. Truly inspiring 🎨✨
⚠️ Disclaimer:
All photos/videos are original content by Travelopedic. Unauthorized use is strictly prohibited.
reviews.author: Harsh Singh

reviews.text:
One of the best villages i have ever visited in all my travels.
The art is phenomenal with each and every one making extremely excellent pieces of creativity.
The art work includes Patachitra, intricate designs on coconut beetle nut .
The work on kettles is complex that it may be taking days to create a single piece.
It's worth a few hours to see the various types of Art available.
reviews.author: Jude Mayne

reviews.text: I wanted to have a Pattachitra painting at home and thought why not get it straight from the source! Delighted to have gone to Raghurajpur. We went to one of the artisans home at night 8 pm and we were welcomed. They even offered us home made chai. The paintings were exquisite work of art; they even mentioned how they made the natural colors from wood apple, lamp black, etc. We bought 2 Pattachitras after negotiating a good price. Would recommend anyone going to Puri to visit Raghurajpur and support the artisans for their craftsmanship; not to mention the soft and polite demeanour of the artists.
reviews.author: Manjit Mohanty

reviews.text: Oh my God.what a artistic village.each and every house is Canvas, there thought, there culture say in art.we visit one  house he is the 7th generation of this art family.we meet his mother, father,and of course his younger son who is only 4year old.The craft men name is Abhimanyu.his art speak louder than the voice.when we ask tell his journey he speaks so humble.as a person he is very calm,polite.
reviews.author: ANUSHKA DAS

reviews.text:
Raghurajpur is a beautiful heritage village where traditional art is preserved and lovingly carried forward from generation to generation. The artists here are extremely welcoming and soft-spoken, patiently explaining their techniques, stories, and the devotion behind each creation.

This is Abhimanyu Bariki with his mother. All the members of his house are involved in Pattachitra paintings, making art not just a profession but a way of life for the entire family.

Their palm leaf engravings and Pattachitra works are undoubtedly superb — filled with intricate detailing, mythological narratives, and graceful compositions. Each painting reflects dedication, precision, and the rich cultural soul of Odisha.

If you are at Puri, dont miss this beautiful places 😊🙏
reviews.author: Medha Naik

simlipal-jungle-lodge.editorial: Sprawling national park known for its picturesque, verdant landscape & elephant reserve.

reviews.text:
Similipal National Park is truly a paradise for nature lovers! 🌿
The dense forests, beautiful waterfalls like Barehipani and Joranda, and the peaceful environment make it a perfect escape from city life. The safari experience was amazing, and we were lucky to spot wildlife including deer and exotic birds.
The greenery, fresh air, and scenic beauty are simply breathtaking. The roads inside the forest are well-maintained, and the staff and guides are helpful and friendly.
If you love adventure and nature, this place is a must-visit in Odisha. Best time to visit is during winter for a pleasant experience.
Highly recommended for friends, families, and photography lovers! 📸
reviews.author: Ajay Kumar

reviews.text: It's designated Tiger Reserve also. Famous for Black Tiger. Though couldn't sight any. Odisha Forest Development Corporation run Ecotourism Complexes are there for Accommodation. Authentic Local Cuisine. Cultural Programs are organised in the Ecotourism Complex for the Guests. Several Waterfalls are there both in the Buffer Zone and Core Zone of the National Park. Situated in the Mayurbhanj District of Odisha
reviews.author: ACHINTYA KUMAR GHOSH

reviews.text: Very good natural forest get away. From Bhuvaneshwar nearly 7 hours drive. From entrance to camp require vehicle with high clearance  like suv . Otherwise u have to spend rs 3000 for suv or can use tiger park vehicle fir nominal rate. Jan morning Safari ride was very chil. Need full winter protection.  0 to 5 degrees now. Good resort staff. Very pleasant service. Cottages are very well maintained. For that remote place  food standards were excellent. We enjoyed  but unlucky could not see any animals
reviews.author: kasthurirangan parthasarathy

reviews.text:
Simlipal Biosphere is a must visit, if you are travelling to Odisha.
Last visited in 1977 as a young Army Officer and had the unique distinction of getting myself photographed with Khiari, the white tigress
From then the tourist facilities have improved leaps and bounds by both the Forest Department as well as the private sector.
Excellent accommodation in the close vicinity with pothole free roads right up to the entrance.
They have numerous safaries at different timings.
You can witness the Santhal tribal dance and enjoy the local cuisine.
reviews.author: Jude Mayne

reviews.text:
I recently visited Similipal National Park, and it was truly a raw and refreshing experience. If you are someone who wants to disconnect from the digital world and reconnect with nature, this place is perfect. There is almost no mobile network inside the forest area and you won’t find nearby shops either, so it’s better to carry all essentials in advance.
The real charm of Similipal lies in its untouched beauty. The kachha roads, dense green trees and pure forest vibes make the journey adventurous and peaceful at the same time. Every turn feels natural and unexplored, giving you a true wildlife and jungle experience.
I stayed at a premium resort with a beautiful river view nearby, which added a touch of comfort to the wild surroundings. Interestingly, while there’s no mobile network in most parts, the resort provides WiFi, so you can stay connected if really needed.
Overall, Similipal is not about luxury tourism. it’s about silence, greenery and raw nature. A perfect getaway for anyone looking to escape city noise and experience real forest life.
reviews.author: biswajit padhi

reviews.text: We had a lovely time at Madhuvan Eco-resort. The staff were friendly and helpful, fully run by women, and treated us like family. The local food was exceptional and many varieties of dishes in generous quantities. The boating trip was great and we managed to see over 20-30 dolphin sightings. Very peaceful spot, away from everything. Also as a bonus, a deer visited us at night and we petted it.
reviews.author: Ronaan Roy

reviews.text: Great option for a one-night stay! The staff is super friendly, and it's run by an NGO with a self-help group of village women - adds to the charm 😊. Food is homely but costly. However, if you're heading to Chilika, try Rajhans Cottages run by the Forest Department, GoO - worth checking out first!
reviews.author: Biranchi Narayan Panda

reviews.text: The stay was nice and the didi’s who were cooking and managing stuffs were really welcoming, we had a great time and a lovely start of the year. Must visit when in Puri! The best part is they are really open to feedback and made us feel belonged
reviews.author: surbhi upadhyay

reviews.text: I loved the hospitality and ambience of the event. Thank you so much for your service. I will recommend everyone to come and reconnect to your innerself.
reviews.author: Sankarsan behera

reviews.text:
The place lacks basic amenities, cleanliness and infrastructure. The toilets were extremely dirty. The water is brown. The doors and windows don’t close. The floor is broken. There are all kinds of insects and lizards. Everything is uncomfortable for a basic stay even for one night.
However, the staff is nice and the food was good and generous (We had lunch there).
reviews.author: Prasad Shetty

reviews.text:
Rooms are not well maintained. It's sad to see such a well located property being wasted away.

reviews.text: A peaceful, cozy stay right in the heart of Ziro! The homestay is clean, comfortable, and surrounded by beautiful views of the valley. reviews.text:
I recently stayed for 3 days at this lovely Japanese-style homestay, and it was a truly peaceful and memorable experience. The atmosphere was calm and authentic, with traditional minimalist décor that made me feel completely immersed in Japanese culture.

The hosts were incredibly kind and welcoming — they made sure I had everything I needed and even offered local travel tips. The room was clean, comfortable, and thoughtfully arranged with all essentials. I especially enjoyed the breakfast served each morning — simple, fresh, and delicious.

The location was convenient yet quiet, perfect for relaxing after a day of sightseeing. The flower garden view added a touch of serenity, and the overall ambiance made the stay feel like home away from home.

I’d definitely recommend this homestay to anyone who wants to experience authentic Japanese hospitality in a cozy and peaceful setting. 🌿

Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)

reviews.text:
I had a wonderful stay at Valley Roots Homestay in Ziro Hari Village. The location is peaceful and surrounded by natural beauty, making it the perfect escape from the noise of the city. The rooms were clean, cozy, and well-maintained, offering both comfort and a homely feeling
The hosts were extremely warm and welcoming treating us like a family
What I loved the most was the connection to nature and the chance to experience the authentic village lifestyle

Overall. Valley Roots Homestay is an ideal place for anyone looking for peace, nature, and genuine hospitality I would highly recommend it to travelers,

reviews.text: The homestay was a delightful experience! The hosts were warm and welcoming, making us feel right at home. reviews.text:

In the middle of nowhere, a place with no piped water, no electricity, is being run with precision by a dedicated group, who cater to the needs  of theirs guests ( mostly birders from all over the world) with a smile on their faces.

reviews.text:
This is a must visit for any bird watching enthusiast.
Situated between tenga and misamari in Arunachal Pradesh. Guwahati to Tenga and then onto the sanctuary is the way to travel.

There are three camps in the sanctuary that offer accommodation. Lama, bompu and khellong.

Hiring an SUV With an experienced driver is recommended as the roads are just jeep tracks in areas

The place is beautiful with mist covered mountains and floating clouds and dense forests

The elevation decreases as you travel from lama to khellong and the flora and fauna changes accordingly.

I have reviewed the accommodation separately
reviews.author: Unnikrishnan G

reviews.text:
Must visit place in Arunachal.

1. Best time to visit: February end to mid of  April
2. How to reach: from Guwahati to Tenga then tenga to Eagle Nest is 44 offroading drive
3. Entry fee : NA
4. Guide Fee: INR 4000 to 6000 if you need a birder guide , but be careful while you deal with the guides.
5. Transportation: You can take your own vehicle to rent a SUV (cost 5000 to 6000 full day). But small hatchback and sedans will face difficulties because of bad roads. So SUV or LUV are prefered.
6. Stay inside sanctuary: Bompu Camp and Lama Camp (cost will be very in  season, usually twin sharing 6000 per night with normal facilities )
7. Environment: Dens forest and very cold during winter.

reviews.text: Best place for bird watcher and Nature lover.  One of my favorite places.The only place where an endangered bird species Bugun Liocichla is found.Views from the top of the camp is amazing. Food and service and the people of the camp were too good.Go for it.

reiek-heritage-village-mizoram.editorial: 1,548-m. mountain featuring trails, a traditional Mizo village & panoramic views of the valley.

reviews.text:
Reiek is one of the most captivating destinations in Mizoram, offering a perfect blend of natural beauty and cultural charm. Nestled about 30 km from Aizawl, this serene hill station is best known for the breathtaking views from Reiek Peak, where rolling green hills stretch endlessly into the horizon. The climb to the top is moderately challenging but rewarding, making it ideal for both adventure seekers and nature lovers.

What makes Reiek truly special is its peaceful atmosphere, far removed from the bustle of city life. The cool, fresh air and the sound of birds create a calming experience that feels almost therapeutic. Visitors can also explore the traditional Mizo heritage village, which offers insight into the local culture, architecture, and way of life.

The area is well-maintained and relatively uncrowded, which adds to its charm. Whether you are trekking, sightseeing, or simply relaxing, Reiek provides a refreshing escape. Overall, it is a must-visit destination for anyone traveling to Mizoram, especially those looking to experience untouched natural beauty and authentic local culture.
reviews.author: TravelwithGhora

reviews.text: It’s a stunning place, filled with beauty of nature, especially in dawn when the sunlight comes out a little the Vally looks more beautiful, the clouds form’s a river 🌄🌄, also in the night you can get a view of milky way from here. This is a must visit place 👍👍.

reviews.text:
Reiek, Mizoram 🌄

Reiek is definitely worth visiting if you’re looking for peace and a quiet escape. The journey from Aizawl to Reiek itself is quite interesting and enjoyable. The place is very calm, less crowded, and perfect to relax. Homestays are available at reasonable prices, making it a great budget-friendly stay with a touch of local hospitality.

reviews.text: For the best experience, it is ideal to visit between September and March, when the weather is cool, the skies are clear, and the views remain unobstructed. Early mornings for sunrise or late afternoons for sunset are particularly mesmerizing, as the golden hour lights up the valley. The base is easily accessible by hiring a cab or renting a two-wheeler from Aizawl, and while the resort offers basic cafeteria facilities and restrooms, you should carry your own water and light snacks for the trek itself. Ultimately, Reiek Tlang offers an incredibly high reward-to-effort ratio, making it an absolute must-visit for nature lovers, photographers, and thrill-seekers alike.

reviews.text:
One of the first 9 tiger reserves of the country,Palamau Tiger Reserve stands as an island of hope for nature and wildlife.
Forest department's unique interventions in habitat restoration,watershed management, hunar se rozgar skill development, eco tourism avenues have infused life back into the tiger landscape, which once suffered huge losses due to long period of Left wing extremism.
The ecotourism sites like Netrahaat,Aksi,lodh,sarnadih,garu,sugga,mirchaiya,buxa,kamaldah qila,betla,kechki provide a bouquet of experience and adventure with decent stay and food  facilities .The open wildlife safari in betla is a thrilling experience,drawing visitors from country and abroad.Patai pattal resturant at buxa mud gouse complex and sunset at kechi sangam beach are unique experiences,which once upon a time was a paradise for esteemed bengali film makers like Satyajit Ray.The pld and new palamau forts of 16th century stand proud in the heart of belta national park bybthe sides of eiver auranga, whispering the stories of the chero kings and the life history of tiger landscape

reviews.text: Excellent place for foreigners looking for studies and education in a true Inia atmosphere. Classes offered on a regular bases. They also have a small hospital clinic for support to the local  community. The food is vegetarian and home made by a wonderful cook.  A wonderful place to clear your heart and head.

reviews.text:
Root institute is a Tibetan Buddhist centre that offers a place to stay, retreat, study, and participate in buddhist courses.

There are a great number of residence buildings on the property for accommodation. Accommodation types available include multi-bed dormitory, triple room, double room, single room with common washroom, or single room with private washroom. Three vegetarian meals are included that are served in the canteen at 7:30AM, 12PM, and 6:30PM.

In February (high season), I booked a single room with a common washroom for 1000 rupees a night. If you stay a month or longer, you can apply a 25% discount.

During the winter season, there are teachers and lamas who come in and either host a week course or give a dhamma teaching during the day. There are also several drop-in events/pujas scheduled here every so often. It appears that anyone is welcome to come and participate in these drop-ins, even some where traditionally I think people would need to have received some initiation first. It appears that isn't important here.

Interesting to note that Root Institute's spiritual guide, Lama Thubten Zopa Rinpoche passed away in 2023, and since has been without any direct successor.

In February it was a bit noisy here at night. This is because there are some venues neighboring areas that appear to host some big events and sometimes play loud music throughout the night. I recommend earplugs for sleeping. Bodhgaya is not specifically the best place to have a quiet environment for meditation though. It is more of a pilgrimage town. Even the lama who recently came to give a teaching here said he doesn't recommend Bodhgaya for finding peace and seclusion. That said, it is still fairly peaceful within the two gompas (tibetan temple) here for meditation during the day.
reviews.author: David Matte

reviews.text: It's a great place to learn,connect and rejoice. Very peaceful. I stayed here for 3 days during my travels across bodhgaya. Got chance to meet very intellectual people who were seeking wisdom. Thanks for maintaining this place.
reviews.author: Neeraj Prabhakar

reviews.text:
We visited the Shakyamuni Buddha Health Care Centre inside the Root Institute campus on 20th November around 12:30 PM, during their lunch break.

Out of curiosity, we (2 people) walked about 30 meters inside the campus. The campus is truly beautiful, peaceful, and filled with an atmosphere that reflects the teachings of Lord Buddha—compassion, calmness, and kindness.

However, the guard from the main gate suddenly came and shouted at us with remarks like “gajab aadmi hain”. He could have simply informed us politely that entry was not allowed. There was no signboard or notice indicating restricted entry.

For a place connected with the values of Buddha, this behaviour felt disappointing. A polite reminder would have carried a much better message in such a serene and spiritual environment.
reviews.author: Rishabh Raj

reviews.text:
Root Institute is an excellent place to stay. Located in a quiet neighborhood of the auspicious birthplace of the Buddha, this FPMT centre offers visitors a quiet place to stay, meditate, contemplate, and be with like-minded community.

I stayed here for 6 weeks in the winter and thoroughly enjoyed my time.

I will note that this region gets cold in the winter, so if you plan to visit between December-February, I suggest you bring plenty of warm clothes. There are markets nearby ot buy afforable blankets and additional layers as well if needed.

A few highlights:
- friendly staff
- spacious location with plenty of greenery and room to wander
- a good rotation of teachings in the winter season
- delicious vegetarian food (the kitchen staff work magic)
- a wealth of knowledge from the staff. Kabir in particular and some of the monastics have been here for decades and share the long history of this place.
- Likely one of the quietiest and most peaceful places to stay in Bodh Gaya.
- cheap and effecrtive laundry service.
reviews.author: Adam Tymko

reviews.text:
Like Wildlife Safari, Nature Safari also crossed my expectations. The way they have built separate areas for different things is commendable. Yes, you have to walk a little extra, so they should have some convenience inside.

From the glass bridge to the suspension bridge to the aviary, everything is top notch and very high quality. Having this kind of experience in Bihar is unbelievable. Highly recommend visiting here.
reviews.author: Pradumna Saraf

reviews.text:
The Rajgir Nature Safari is much more than just a wildlife park—it’s a complete adventure destination nestled within the stunning Rajgir hills! I was completely charmed by the blend of natural beauty and exhilarating activities this complex offers.
The entire area is a beautifully maintained, clean, and green paradise, perfect for a day out with family or friends. Beyond the wildlife zones, the true standout features for me were the adventure activities. Walking across the Glass Bridge offered breathtaking, panoramic views of the hills, while the Sky Walk and Zip Line provided an adrenaline rush I wasn't expecting!
The design is world-class, making it easy to spend a full day here. With well-paved pathways, clean rest areas, and cozy cafeterias, the focus is clearly on visitor comfort. This location truly leverages the scenic backdrop of the Rajgir hills, making every viewpoint a photo opportunity. It’s an essential visit for anyone seeking relaxation, beautiful scenery, and a dose of adventure while traveling in Bihar.
⭐ Essential Tip: Remember to secure your spot for this popular destination! Tickets must be booked online 3 to 4 days in advance via the official website to ensure entry.it is wise to book ticket online as offline ticket often finished earlier
reviews.author: aparna chakdar

reviews.text: Had a great time at Rajgir Nature Safari! I really enjoyed the place — lots of fun activities and everything is affordable. The food was good and reasonably priced too. My favorite part was the zip line, it was amazing! The glass bridge was okay, but it didn’t meet my expectations. Overall, a fun and budget-friendly experience.

reviews.text: Must Visit Place specially for Bharat Ka Second Longest Zip Line and The Unique Glass Bridge. Make sure to have the ticket in advance either through online or from the ticket counter.

parmarth-niketan-rishikesh.editorial: Spacious Himalayan ashram with lush gardens, offering courses in yoga, meditation & Ayurveda.

reviews.text: I had an absolutely wonderful experience at Parmarth Niketan Ashram! The atmosphere was incredibly peaceful and serene, and the views of the Ganges at night were breathtaking. 🌙 The grand Shiva statue really stood out, making it a very spiritual visit. 🙏 I felt so much tranquility there. Definitely recommend visiting on a weekend! ✨🧘
reviews.author: Smit Dwivedi

reviews.text:
After hearing plenty of positive feedback about Parmarth Niketan, I recently visited this spiritual site located on the banks of the sacred Ganga near Janki bridge.

The first thing that stands out is its cleanliness; even the restrooms are sparkling. The area is filled with trees, statues, and a fountain, creating a tranquil atmosphere throughout. There are many stunning views to capture, which we certainly did. The ashram is renowned for its spiritual education, yoga classes, meditation, and social initiatives, particularly its cleanliness drives, educational efforts for underprivileged children, and addressing various social concerns.

Another highlight is the restaurant named Hridyam, which boasts a lovely ambiance and a vibrant atmosphere. The menu offers a wide range of options, from snacks to main courses and bakery items.

The surrounding area is just as impressive, featuring several showrooms and a bustling street market, alongside the breathtaking view of Janki bridge and the sunset above it.

I believe no trip to Rishikesh is complete without a visit to this place. This place welcomes everyone, regardless of caste or creed.
reviews.author: Sudesh K Jain

reviews.text:
My visit to Parmarth Niketan Ashram was truly a soulful and calming experience. The moment I reached the ashram, I could instantly feel a deep sense of peace and positivity in the surroundings. The serene location on the banks of the holy Ganga, with the Himalayan foothills in the background, creates an atmosphere that naturally pulls you away from daily stress and noise.
I visited the ashram on my bike, and one important thing I would like to mention for fellow travelers is do not park your vehicle in front of the ashram, as the traffic police impose fines. It’s better to park at designated areas and walk peacefully to the ashram.
Although I couldn’t get a chance to stay in the ashram or taste their sattvik food this time, I was fortunate enough to attend the Sandhya Ganga Aarti, which was a deeply spiritual and mesmerizing experience. The rhythmic chants, lamps glowing on the river, and devotional bhajans created an environment filled with divine energy. I also noticed many international visitors completely immersed in meditation and bhajans, which beautifully reflects how this place connects people beyond cultures and borders.
The overall vibe of Parmarth Niketan is pure, disciplined, and spiritually uplifting. The cleanliness, organization, and peaceful flow of daily activities reflect the values the ashram stands for. Even if you are not staying here, the evening Ganga Aarti is free and a must-attend experience when visiting Rishikesh.
This place is not just an ashram; it is a journey inward. Highly recommended for anyone seeking peace, devotion, and a meaningful spiritual experience.
Har Har Mahadev 🙏
reviews.author: Arun Shankar

reviews.text:
Attending the Ganga Maa Aarti at Parmarth Niketan Ashram is a truly divine and unforgettable experience. Set on the peaceful banks of the holy Ganga in Rishikesh, the evening aarti fills the atmosphere with spirituality, devotion, and positive energy.
As the sun sets, the sound of bhajans, rhythmic chanting, and the sight of beautifully lit diyas create a mesmerizing ambiance. The ceremony is well-organized, and the involvement of devotees and young students adds a special charm to the entire experience. Watching the priests perform the rituals with such grace and devotion brings a deep sense of peace and connection.
The serene surroundings, combined with the spiritual vibrations, make it a perfect place to reflect and feel inner calm. Whether you are religious or simply seeking a soulful experience, the Ganga Aarti at Parmarth Niketan is something you should not miss when visiting Rishikesh.
reviews.author: Manish Singh Soun

reviews.text: Amazing and mesmerising, no words can describe the stay. I visited with parents and it was an amazing experience till date. Devine and spiritual atmosphere all around. All daily activities are full of energy. For stay, Kindly book properly through official email only. Aashram is not accepting any bookings on direct website or phone or WhatsApp, don't come into fraud trap. Ganga Aarti at the evening is free to attend even if you are not staying. MUST Visit place if you are visiting Rishikesh. Har Har Bhole 🙏🏻

reviews.text:

reviews.author: רומי לוי

reviews.text:
My days at Phool Chatti Ashram were truly life-changing.
Surrounded by the sacred Ganga, the calm of the Himalayas, and the peaceful rhythm of the ashram, I experienced a deep sense of clarity and inner silence.

The daily schedule here is perfectly balanced — yoga, meditation, silence, chanting, and connection with nature.
The food is pure, sattvic, and served with so much love that it nourishes both body and soul.
Everyone at the ashram — from the kitchen staff to the teachers — radiates kindness and warmth.

My heartfelt gratitude to Lalita Ji and Meenakshi Madam.
Their guidance, compassion, and patience helped me see spirituality in a new light.
They showed me how simple living, mindfulness, and love can bring deep peace and transformation.

Phool Chatti is not just a retreat; it is a journey back to yourself — a reminder of what truly matters.
Thank you to the entire team for creating such a sacred and healing space. 🙏

— Yuvraj Nayar

reviews.text: phool chatti was the first Ashram experience for me! it is a perfect place , especially for beginners with friendly routine , delicious wholesome food , warm homely and family like environment from the organisers , staff and teachers ... serene views , adventure treks , opportunity of selfless service , and varieties of yoga , meditation activities... truly gives a practical insight into ashram style living and yogic life! peace , purity and love that is the vibe!

reviews.text:
Konnichiwa 🙏
I completed my 200 Hours Yoga Teacher Training Course and Sound Healing Course at Yogamani, and it was a very beautiful and meaningful experience for me.

I attended the course with a Japanese batch, which made the learning environment very comfortable and supportive. The teachers were kind, patient, and very helpful. They explained everything in simple and clear English, so it was easy to understand.

The yoga classes helped me improve my practice, flexibility, and confidence. The sound healing sessions were very peaceful and relaxing, helping me feel calm and stress-free.

The accommodation was clean, quiet, and comfortable, and the food was also good.

I am very grateful for this experience and highly recommend Yogamani for yoga teacher training and sound healing courses.
Arigatou 🙏

reviews.text: We had booked a 3BHK apartment in this residence through Booking.com. About three hours before check-in, they asked us to pay an advance of ₹1000, which we paid through GPay. When we went to the service apartment and met the caretaker, he gave us the key to a 2BHK room. We asked why he was giving us a 2BHK key when we had booked a 3BHK, and he insisted that we had booked a 2BHK.Later, after speaking with his superior, we were told that the 3BHK room we had booked was not yet vacated and that we should take the 2BHK instead. With no other option left, we agreed. The management never informed us in advance that the booked room was unavailable; if they had, we would have booked elsewhere. They gave us two mattresses but did not provide pillows, blankets, or bedspreads. The kitchen had no utensils or cooking equipment, and we had to ask for each item individually. When we requested stainless steel tumblers, they gave us plastic ones. There were eight of us, but they provided only four. Overall, we were very disappointed and distressed by this booking experience. There was also no dining table for eating. The bathroom did not have any soap, shampoo and towels. There was no cupboard in the room either. After when we were checking out, the caretaker asked for a tips.

kanger-valley-nature-camp.editorial: Forested landscape with limestone caves & waterfalls, home to Bastar mynah birds & crocodiles.

reviews.text:
The beauty is undeniable.Kanger Valley National Park, located in the Bastar region of Chhattisgarh, is a hidden gem that showcases the untouched beauty of central India’s forests. This national park is a perfect destination for nature lovers, adventure seekers, and those looking to escape the noise of city life. Surrounded by dense sal and teak forests, calm streams, limestone caves, and rich wildlife, Kanger Valley offers a truly immersive natural experience.
One of the most striking aspects of Kanger Valley National Park is its serene atmosphere. The slow-flowing rivers and crystal-clear water bodies reflect the surrounding trees, creating a peaceful and soothing environment. Activities like boating or kayaking, as seen in the park, allow visitors to enjoy nature up close while maintaining harmony with the ecosystem. The silence here is broken only by the sounds of flowing water, birds, and rustling leaves, making it an ideal place for relaxation and mindfulness.
The park is also famous for its natural caves, such as Kutumsar and Kailash caves, which attract tourists and researchers alike. These limestone caves are geological wonders and add a sense of mystery and adventure to the visit. Wildlife enthusiasts may spot deer, wild boar, leopards, and a wide variety of birds, making it a great destination for photography and birdwatching.
What truly makes Kanger Valley special is its raw and less-commercialized charm. The area still feels pure and close to nature, offering an authentic forest experience. Overall, Kanger Valley National Park is a must-visit destination in Chhattisgarh—peaceful, scenic, and rich in biodiversity. It beautifully reflects the natural and cultural heritage of Bastar and leaves visitors with lasting memories of tranquility and wonder.

the-machan-lonavala.editorial: Rustic treehouses & cabins in an environmentally conscious property offering a spa & birding.

reviews.text:
I had a truly wonderful stay at Machan. Tucked beautifully in the lap of nature, it offers an exceptional forest experience that feels peaceful and rejuvenating. The crisp, clear skies and incredibly clean air made it a perfect escape from the hustle and bustle of city life.

The food and beverage services at the restaurants were excellent, with great quality and warm hospitality throughout. A special compliment to Vikas and his entire team for managing the property so wonderfully. Vikas personally took our feedback very positively and seemed genuinely committed to doing even better each time. His attentiveness and strong focus on customer service truly stand out, and I’m sure his passion will take this beautiful property to even greater heights.

It was a fabulous getaway, and I genuinely look forward to visiting again soon with my husband.
reviews.author: Neesha Kakar

reviews.text: What can I say about The Machan!!! It is a beautiful place to relax and rediscover yourself away from the hustle and bustle of daily life. With the option of not buying internet we were able to truly relax and enjoy ourselves. We walked a lot and just sat and chilled or read books. The landscape is amazing and very beautifully maintained with a variety of flowers and plants. You can hear the birds chirping all morning!! Or watch their cute dogs run around and playing with each other. The Machans make you feel like you are floating in the sky. We lived in the Canopy 3 machan, above the tree lines. View from it was gorgeous. They are built with great care to not disturb nature but also are sturdy. Inside too is beautiful with handcrafted furniture from the locals. Every member of their staff is courteous, polite and helpful and all seem truly happy. Food is excellent with good variety each meal. Breakfast, lunch, and dinner are served at 3 different locations daily, each with its own beauty, so the need to order room service was not an option for us. We walked a lot in the jungles on very well marked trails and just enjoyed the serenity it offered. Very safe. Never left the grounds. We enjoyed massages on 2 separate days and are glad we did so! Relaxing!! We took a few guided tours and were very impressed with the guides level of knowledge. He knew his stuff!! Strongly recommend this place to visit. You will truly see a gem in India.
reviews.author: seema chouhan

reviews.text: Love the hospitality, love the nature, love the silence and love the people. The beginning had a little hiccup but Vikas made sure that the entire experience will be special and memorable. I am glad we had all the help we needed and correct guidance to make the best of our stay. Also Digambar had made sure that we didn’t have to worry about anything and always present at our service. Love his attitude and politeness and helping nature for the guests. Do ask for him if you want to experience royalty.
reviews.author: Riya Deepsi

reviews.text: We had a wonderful time on our first visit. The property is truly stunning — serene, beautifully maintained, and offering a sense of calm the moment you arrive. The ambiance is warm and inviting, making it the perfect getaway to unwind. The staff are incredibly welcoming, attentive, and intuitive, always anticipating your needs with genuine care. A special mention to Ganesh, Vikas and Vino for their thoughtful assistance, even insisting on carrying our bag. Their kindness truly enhanced our stay. Exceptional hospitality all around. We would highly recommend this place and look forward to returning in the near future. Thank you very much.
reviews.author: Damaris D'cunha

reviews.text:
Our experience at The Machan was just okay. The rooms were good and comfortable, the food was average and we appreciate the service provided by Joseph and Prabhakar, who were courteous and helpful , that’s about where the positives end.

Considering the prices charged, the overall experience did not feel luxurious. Almost every activity on the property comes at an additional cost. Only yoga and a documentary were complimentary, which is frankly unacceptable at this price point. There are only two free bicycles for the entire property, no game room, and very limited recreational options unless you’re willing to keep paying extra.

While the resort promotes a “disconnect from the world” concept, which we actually liked, as there is no mobile network, the decision to charge extra for Wi-Fi in the rooms felt contradictory and unreasonable. Another downside was that the prices are not inclusive of taxes. Even when we were offered a 20% discount on the spa, an 18% tax was added on top, which significantly reduced the benefit of the discount.

Overall, the experience did not live up to expectations, especially for the price paid. With so many add-on costs, it didn’t feel like good value for money.

reviews.text:
We stayed during 13th till 15th June 2025 last year. Rooms were clean and food was good too. But there 2 major issues we faced during our stay :-

1) Despite booking their second best category room i.e, Brick villa, the AC was not working starting from the beginning. Prince, the caretaker and other staff were not able to resolve or identify this issue during our entire stay. The issue was there was some gas leakage in AC due to which there was hardly any cooling inside the room in peak summers and we were sweating whole night. They should ideally check these issues before the guests arrive. When I reported the same issue to the owner on whatsapp, he just said “OK” and there was no apology/call or any other efforts done to resolve this issue

2) Service was slow most of the times. Prince and other staff were busy serving and attending a VIP guest (VIP as per them), some SDM, on 15th June 2025. Due to this they delayed our breakfast by more than an hour since they were busy attending him. We  got late for our next journey. And when they finally brought the breakfast, it was not served hot
